jobs.title: Global Investment Banking – M&A Internship (September - December), Frankfurt
jobs.description:
About Baird

Founded in 1919, Baird has more than 4,500 associates and offices in Europe and the United States. We deliver investment banking, private equity and equity research services to institutions and corporations around the world.

Our unique culture was founded on the values of integrity, transparency, teamwork and putting our clients first, principles that underpin our strong relationships with our clients and our communities to this day.

Being privately held and employee-owned allows to us focus squarely on our clients’ needs. And, our recognition as a great place to work enables us to attract and retain exceptional professionals who share a passion for delivering great outcomes for our clients.

About Baird Global Investment Banking

With our mid-market focus, deep expertise and unique team approach, Baird’s Global Investment Banking group provides comprehensive and global M&A, debt advisory and equity financing services to companies, entrepreneurs, and private equity and venture capital firms around the world.

Our European team operates across a range of industries and subsectors but specialises in the Consumer, Healthcare, Industrial, and Technology & Services sectors.

Our in-depth industry and product knowledge, combined with extensive transaction experience and global financial sponsor coverage, enable us to consistently achieve high-quality, successful outcomes for our clients.

This PAID internship is offered for a duration of 3 months and will start on Monday, 28 September 2026 until Friday, 18 December 2026.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support with the preparation of marketing materials for pitches, buy- and sell-side transactions, and other client meetings

  • Prepare company valuations and financial analyses using a variety of valuation methodologies

  • Perform company and market research and prepare accompanying analyses

  • Assist deal-teams with the execution of live buy- and sell-side transactions

Qualifications

  • Excellent academic record from a top-tier university

  • High level of motivation and capacity for teamwork

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in both English and German

  • Strong quantitative and analytical skills including advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills

  • Strong work ethic and ability to work well under pressure and to tight deadlines

  • Passion for excellence and attention to detail

  • Experience in M&A or other financial services is preferential

Additional Information

  • Internships are offered for a duration of 3 months

  • Successful interns will be considered for full-time positions to join the incoming Analyst class

  • Intern positions are filled on a rolling basis therefore we encourage you to apply as soon as possible

To apply, please submit your CV, cover letter and references. If uploading multiple files, please drag and drop them under the ‘Resume / Cover Letter’ tab.

Baird is committed to diversity and provides employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, citizenship, national origin, age, disability, military service, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, or any other status protected by law.

jobs.title: Equity Capital Markets Analyst
jobs.description:
Note: This specific posting may have been filled or removed. Browse current openings at Rwbaird careers.

High-profile deals, major growth, the right people behind you. Grow far at Baird. ​

Baird offers a different kind of start to a career in investment banking, built on high-performing teams and a culture that supports your growth. ​ Here, you’ll build skills through real transactions, close collaboration with peers and mentors, and a culture focused on shared success.​

​

​What You’ll Do​

Learn by doing and be trusted with meaningful work from the jump​

  • Provide analytical support on public offerings, financing transactions and other financial advisory services for our clients

  • Contribute to business development efforts through research, analysis, and preparation for client meetings and pitches​

  • Work side-by-side with both junior and senior investment bankers, gaining hands-on experience across every stage of a deal ​

  • Partner across the investment banking and sales & trading platforms to drive transaction advisory and deal execution

  • Use AI tools like Rogo and auxi to streamline the creation of pitch books and models, and employ data visualization software like Power BI to generate data-driven insights and reports for client materials

What You’ll Gain​

The kind of environment that turns early talent into lasting careers​

  • Contribute to every stage of a transaction, from conducting research to facilitating due diligence​

  • Gain insight into how teams collaborate across geographies, sectors, and specialties to support client objectives​

  • Take on meaningful responsibility and contribute to conversations with investor and corporate management clients around the world​

  • Grow in a culture built on respect and shared values, where ambition and collaboration go hand-in-hand and hard work is recognized through opportunity​

What It Takes​

We’re looking for people who want to go far, and go together​

  • A candidate energized by learning, teamwork, and real responsibility​

  • 12–24 months of full-time work experience (investment banking preferred)​

  • A proven academic record and a passion for finance (a finance degree is helpful, but not required)​

  • Drive, resilience, and a strong work ethic​

  • Ability to work independently and thrive in a collaborative setting​

  • Strong mathematical, writing, and verbal skills; relevant software proficiency​

  • SIE and Series 79 licensing preferred (or the ability to obtain them quickly)​

  • Experience with AI tools such as Rogo, CoPilot, and auxi is preferred

  • Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar

Compensation and Benefits:

  • $110,000 - $125,000 annual salary with bonus potential

  • Compensation and bonus are commensurate with experience, performance and/or GIB profitability

In addition to a strong culture that is focused on health and well-being, our organization offers competitive total compensation packages and comprehensive benefits including: protected Friday night and Saturdays, periodic sabbaticals during career, shareholder opportunities, mandatory paid time off (PTO) and robust 401k match and profit-sharing contribution

Note to External Recruiters / Search Firms: Baird does not accept unsolicited resumes and will not compensate for any placement resulting from such submissions. Any unsolicited resumes received will not be treated as valid applications.

Baird is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Baird is committed to diversity and provides employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, citizenship, national origin, age, disability, military service, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, or any other status protected by law.

jobs.title: Equity Derivatives Sales Trader
jobs.description:
About the Role:
Baird is seeking an experienced and highly motivated Equity Derivatives Sales Trader to join our Institutional Sales & Trading team in New York. This role is ideal for a proven options trader with strong market acumen, sound judgment, and the ability to build and sustain trusted client relationships. The successful candidate will thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment while contributing to the continued growth of Baird’s equity derivatives platform.

The Impact You’ll Make:

  • Drive commission revenue by leveraging client relationships, market insight, trading ideas, and the broader Baird platform

  • Serve as a subject matter expert by educating clients and internal client-facing teams on equity options capabilities

  • Develop, communicate, and execute trading strategies informed by market trends, volatility dynamics, and underlying asset fundamentals

  • Monitor market conditions, regulatory developments, and emerging trends within the equity derivatives space

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with brokers, counterparties, and other market participants

What You’ll Bring to Baird:

  • 3–7 years of successful experience in equity derivatives trading, with a strong focus on options

  • FINRA licenses: SIE, Series 7, 57 and 63 active and Series 4 completed within 6 months of start date.

  • Demonstrated ability to build and maintain strong industry relationships, with potential for transferable business considered a plus

  • Deep understanding of options pricing models, volatility dynamics, and risk management techniques

  • Comprehensive knowledge of equity and derivative markets, including underlying fundamentals and macroeconomic drivers

  • Proven ability to make sound, timely decisions in fast-paced, high-pressure trading environments

  • Strong communication skills with the ability to clearly articulate complex trading concepts and strategies

  • Experience using industry-standard options trading platforms and risk management systems

  • Strong quantitative, mathematical, and statistical skills, including the ability to develop and apply quantitative trading models

Compensation and Total Rewards Package:

  • $150,000 -$200,000 annual salary range

  • Bonus potential annually

  • Compensation and bonus are commensurate with experience, performance and/or firm profitability

  • Check out ourTotal Rewards at Baird, which is a summary of our benefits and compensation

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Davidson Investment Advisors is seeking a Client Portfolio Manager to join our growing team. This role is an exciting opportunity to help serve and expand a key segment of our business while contributing to the strong culture of our firm. 
Summary: 
The Client Portfolio Manager plays a critical role in serving nonprofit, endowment, corporate, insurance, and charitable trust clients by helping them structure, manage, and steward their investment portfolios. This position offers the opportunity to work closely with our business development, investment, and operations teams to deliver exceptional client experiences rooted in integrity, professionalism, and a deep understanding of financial markets.
Ideal candidates bring strong communication skills, a passion for mission aligned investing, and the ability to build trust with complex institutional clients. While this role is primarily client service focused, it also plays an important part in driving the ongoing growth and refinement of this business segment.
 Qualifications/Requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, business or a related field
• 5+ years of experience in financial services, insurance, banking, or nonprofit leadership
• Ability to work independently, analyze complex problems, and provide thoughtful, solutions-oriented recommendations
• Strong understanding of investment strategies and best practices for institutional and nonprofit clients
• Excellent organization and proficiency with Client Relationship Management systems
• High proficiency in Microsoft Office 
• Self-starter with excellent organizational and communication skills, with the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment effectively
• Detail-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability 
• Collaborative mindset with a commitment to contributing to team success.
Duties: 
• Provide exceptional client service to institutional clients of the firm
o Prepare and present to boards of directors and other decision makers
o Maintain consistent communication with staff and board members regarding market conditions, portfolio strategies, performance, and potential enhancements
o Collaborate with investment and operations teams to align investment strategies and operational deliverables with client needs
o Monitor client portfolios to ensure ongoing compliance with Investment Policies 
• Implement investment strategies for clients that align with their objectives and risk tolerances
• Prospect, pitch, and convert new institutional clients to our firm’s services
• Work closely with D.A. Davidson Financial Advisors to deliver integrated solutions for shared clients
• Drive initiatives to enhance efficiency and optimize workflows; proactively seek ways to improve the team’s processes
• Perform any other duties and special projects as necessary Adhere to all the firm’s Code of Ethics and Policies and Procedures, as well as its Team Agreement, which is a commitment to the following behaviors:
o Curiosity (avoid becoming defensive and remaining open to learning)
o Accountability (taking 100% responsibility (no more; no less) for results; no blaming)
o Candor (engaging in courageous truth-telling to increase power and speed in decisions)
o Awareness (tapping every emotional and intuitional resource for decisions)
o Passion (discovering and allowing people to do what they do best)
o Appreciation (expressing gratitude and fostering a culture of sincere cooperation)
What we offer:
Competitive salary plus excellent benefits and perks including, but not limited to:  
• Medical, Dental and Vision
• Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
• Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
• Paid holidays
• Professional Development Opportunities
• Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
• Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
• Charitable gift-matching program
• Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.  
The compensation offered will be determined on a case-by-case basis considering a variety of factors including, but not limited to, the skills, relevant work experience, and geographic location of each specific candidate.

jobs.title: Equity Capital Markets – Vice President
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.

D.A. Davidson & Co., a leading investment banking firm, is accepting applicants for a full-time Vice President position in our Equity Capital Markets group based in New York City. The Vice President is expected to project manage strategic assignments assisting clients with public offerings, private placements and other advisory services. The Vice President is expected to translate Director and Managing Director wishes into achievable tasks, assign projects to Associates and Analysts and see these projects through to completion. The Vice President is expected to be a coach to junior bankers and build relationships with clients.  

Qualifications:

  • Strong transaction history – Capital Markets experience preferred
  • Ability to lead transaction execution teams.
  • High degree of comfort interacting with senior executives.
  • Strong financial modeling ability and Microsoft Office expertise.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Superior work ethic and teamwork skills.
  • Good judgment and ability to exercise discretion when handling confidential information.
  • Ability to perform in both team and self-directed settings.
  • MBA with at least 3 years of capital markets experience or undergraduate degree with a minimum of 6 years capital markets experience.
  • Possession of Series 79 (Series 7 may be applicable) and 63 licenses preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will be required upon hiring.

In this role you will:

  • Lead the day-to-day execution of transactions (Public Offerings / Private Placements), including preparing and reviewing company marketing documents and reviewing financial models, managing marketing and coordinating due diligence, analyzing financing alternatives and negotiating documentation.
  • Establish client trust by identifying needs, project leadership, transaction structuring, company positioning, due diligence, negotiating, etc.
  • Assist senior bankers in the business development efforts.
  • Contribute to revenue targets for deal teams through relationship development and identifying deal opportunities for the firm.
  • Manage, train and develop Associates and Analysts on the team.
  • Ensure quality collaboration and production of work flow and product from Associates and Analysts.

Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.
Investment Banking – Experienced Analyst
D.A. Davidson & Co., a leading full-service investment banking firm with operations nationwide, is accepting applicants for full-time Analyst positions working with the firm’s Investment Banking Diversified Industrials Group in our Boston, MA office. The Experienced Analyst will play an important role working with senior bankers on strategic assignments, assisting clients with mergers and acquisitions, public offerings, private placements and other advisory services. 

Qualifications:

  • Bright, professional, and motivated with the desire to excel in an intellectually challenging environment
  • 1-3 years of experience working in investment banking or transaction experience in related fields such as accounting, consulting or corporate finance required.
  • Interest and curiosity in the Diversified Industrials sector
  • Bachelor’s degree with a minimum 3.5 GPA.
  • Working knowledge of business software (especially Word, Excel and PowerPoint).
  • High level of financial knowledge (financial modeling, macroeconomics, current events, etc.).
  • Excellent verbal and written communications skills, strong work ethic and teamwork skills. Self-motivated, with a strong desire to excel in a challenging field.
  • Good judgment in confidential situations and ability to exercise discretion when handling confidential information.
  • Flexibility to work long hours, nights, and weekends, as needed for workflow.
  • Possession of Securities Industries Essentials (SIE), Series 79, and 63 licenses preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will require successful completion upon hiring.

In this role you will:

  • Develop financial modules to analyze a wide variety of client-specific projects.
  • Collaborate with an industry team from peer analysts to managing directors.
  • Prepare research/analytical content for pitch books, memorandums, presentations, and other projects.
  • Support business development activities of senior investment bankers.
  • Attend client meetings and interact on transactional and technical issues.
  • Actively participate in live deals, such as M&A, IPOs, and advisory.

jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.
D.A. Davidson’s investment banking division is a leading full-service investment bank that offers comprehensive financial advisory and capital markets expertise for public and private companies, including M&A, equity and debt offerings. The group has extensive transaction experience serving middle market clients worldwide across four industry verticals: Consumer, Diversified Industrials, Financial Institutions, and Technology. Together with its European strategic partner, MCF Corporate Finance, D.A. Davidson originates and executes transatlantic M&A transactions under the common brand of D.A. Davidson MCF International.
D.A. Davidson Companies is an employee-owned financial services firm offering a range of financial services and advice to individuals, corporations, institutions and municipalities nationwide. Founded in 1935 and headquartered in Montana, with investment banking offices in Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Denver, Great Falls, New York, Orange County, Portland and Salt Lake City, the company has approximately 1,400 employees and offices in 27 states. 
Investment Banking – Experienced Associate 1 or 2 
D.A. Davidson & Co. is accepting applicants for Associate Year 1 and 2 positions in the Technology Investment Banking Group. The Associate will play a vital role working with senior bankers on strategic assignments, assisting clients with mergers and acquisitions, public offerings, private placements and other advisory services.
Associates start on a four-year program with a deep dive into their industry of focus, receive mentorship and sales and public speaking training. Associates are encouraged to assist in recruiting, college outreach, cultural initiatives and ongoing analyst training.

Qualifications:

  • Bright, professional, and motivated with the desire to excel in an intellectually challenging environment
  • Three years minimum of professional experience working in investment banking, private equity, corporate development, etc. is required
  • Bachelor’s degree
  • Highly motivated, confident, professional, and coachable
  • Proficiency with Capital IQ and PitchBook is a plus
  • Advanced knowledge of business software (especially Word, Excel and PowerPoint)
  • Strong knowledge of financial modeling, corporate finance and accounting
  • Excellent verbal and written communications skills including business writing
  • Able to handle multiple projects with competing priorities
  • Strong work ethic and team oriented approach
  • Good judgment in confidential situations and ability to exercise discretion
  • High degree of comfort and confidence interacting with senior executives
  • Ability to train and provide mentorship to analysts and interns
  • Flexibility to work long hours, nights, and weekends, as needed
  • Securities Industry Essentials Exam, Series 79 (Series 7 may be applicable) and 63 licenses strongly preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will require successful completion upon hiring 

In this role you will:

  • Work closely with senior bankers to support business development, sourcing and execution of transactions
  • Actively participate in live deals, such as M&A, capital offerings and strategic advisory
  • Supervise investment banking analysts, including mentoring and oversight of the work product
  • Take the lead for developing important technical analysis and presentations
  • Create marketing materials
  • Communicate and interact with clients in executing transactions
  • Travel is required (i.e. client meetings, conferences, etc.)
  • Participate in recruiting efforts

jobs.title: Managed Assets Research Associate
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Summary/Function: 
D.A. Davidson & Co.’s Managed Assets Research Department provides a multitude of services for our firm’s advisors and their clients. The research team is responsible for developing and maintaining recommendations across the mutual fund, ETF and Separately Managed Account (SMA) landscape. Further, this research department is also responsible for managing a wide array of asset allocation portfolios on a discretionary basis for our firm’s advisors and their clients. Communications with our financial advisors are primarily provided through quarterly presentations, model portfolio reports, client portfolio reviews, conference calls, and availability by direct phone or email for engagement with our advisors. 
The primary function of the Research Associate position is to support the overall activities of the Managed Assets Research department. We are looking for an associate with an organic interest in the financial markets and investments, who is motivated by the prospect of playing a vital role in delivering high level of service and quality investment solutions to our advisors and their clients. 
Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in economics, business, marketing or related field required. 
• Series 7 & 65/66, or willingness to obtain.
• Progress toward, or desire to pursue, the CFA or CAIA charter designation also viewed positively.
• 2+ years of experience in the financial services industry, preferably in an environment of working with financial advisors.
• Strong writing and proofreading skills.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel, Word and PowerPoint. Experience with Adobe Acrobat, and/or Morningstar Direct a significant plus.
• Strong communication skills, written and verbal, with excellent organizational skills.
• Articulate, confident, driven professional with the ability to work well independently, and as part of a cohesive team in a fast-paced environment. 
Duties:
• Provide research and marketing support, as needed, to the department’s director and broader team.
• Assist in the continued development of the research team’s communications process and procedures.  This consists of, but is not limited to:
o Proofreading/editing text, creating supporting charts & tables, and ultimately distributing presentations and reports through different mediums.
o Maintaining and developing investment reports and note templates.
o Maintain and continue to develop the Managed Assets Research intranet site, internal department databases, and other communications. 
o Keeping organized files for our internal and external publications and other department activities, as appropriate.
• Respond to frequent ad hoc research requests from financial advisors, typically by phone and/or email.
• Assist the research team in client portfolio reviews and proposals. Recommendations in these reviews and proposals often utilize the team’s asset allocation portfolios and selections from our Supervised and Approved Research lists for Mutual Funds, ETF’s and SMA’s. 
What we offer:
Competitive salary plus excellent benefits and perks including, but not limited to:  
•Medical, Dental and Vision
•Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
•Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
•Paid holidays
•Professional Development Opportunities
•Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
•Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
•Charitable gift-matching program
•Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.  
The potential base pay hiring range for this role is $90,000 – 110,000 annually. The compensation offered will be determined on a case-by-case basis considering a variety of factors including, but not limited to, the skills, relevant work experience, and geographic location of each specific candidate.
D.A. Davidson has been in business for over 90 years! As an employee-owned company, our success is driven by our high standards of business ethics, integrity and the belief that our Associates are our most important assets.  We hope you will consider joining us!

jobs.title: Quantitative Analyst, Public Finance
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.

Summary/Function: 

The Quantitative Analyst supports Fixed Income Capital Markets by providing financial modeling, analytical support, pricing, and quantitative insights within the Development Finance Group for public finance and structured transactions. This role is hands-on, production-oriented, and designed for an individual capable of operating as a senior contributor within the quant group and investment banking team.  This role is designed for individuals with modeling skills for debt transactions who is ready to work with the Development Finance Team on financings across the nation. 

The position partners closely with bankers, traders, and senior leadership to support deal execution, debt service analysis, structuring, and client deliverables.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field
  • 3+ years of experience in public finance, fixed income, structured products, or quantitative financial analysis
  • Demonstrated ability to function as a senior or near senior quant without extensive ramp up
  • Strong understanding of municipal bond structures, debt service mechanics, and pricing conventions
  • Advanced proficiency in Excel (complex formulas, scenarios, DBC experience is a plus)
  • Experience with financial modeling software, databases, or coding is beneficial
  • Ability to manage multiple live priorities in deadline-driven environments
  • Strong analytical and quantitative reasoning skills
  • High attention to detail, accuracy, and internal quality control
  • Ability to communicate complex analyses clearly to non-quant stakeholders
  • Sound judgment under pressure
  • Collaborative mindset with strong service orientation to bankers and clients
  • Professional discretion and reliability
  • Experience supporting development finance, housing, public finance, or specialty finance sectors
  • Interest in growing into broader responsibilities over time

Duties:

  • Build, audit, and maintain cash flows for debt models, pricing models, and sensitivity analysis for transactions
  • Support new-issue pricing, refunding analyses, and scenario modeling across client deals
  • Assist bankers with structuring solutions, yield calculations, debt service schedules, and alternative financing comparisons
  • Provide quantitative support during live transactions, including rapid-turn analyses under tight deadlines
  • Incorporate feedback from traders and bankers to evaluate market conditions, investor demand, and relative value
  • Review, analyze, and validate assumptions used in offering materials and client presentations
  • Support ongoing internal tools, templates, and model enhancements to improve efficiency and consistency
  • Collaborate across quant and banking teams to balance workload during peak periods
  • Ensure analytical work is accurate and meets internal and regulatory expectations

Why this position:

  • High-visibility seat within Fixed Income Capital Markets with a growing and dynamic team specializing in Development Finance nationwide.
  • Direct exposure to live transactions, senior banking, and senior leadership
  • Opportunity to make immediate impact during a period of strong deal flow and demand

jobs.title: Vice President, Fixed Income Trading
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Position Overview
We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Vice President, Fixed Income Trader with deep expertise in taxable bonds to join our institutional trading desk. This role has a primary emphasis on corporate bond trading, while also requiring a foundational understanding of mortgage-backed securities (MBS) and agency securities. The ideal candidate will possess a strong grasp of credit markets, trading strategies, and risk management, and will contribute meaningfully to trade execution, idea generation, and overall desk profitability.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Execute trades in investment-grade and high-yield corporate bonds across primary and secondary markets.
  • Execute trades in other taxable asset classes as needed.
  • Provide market color and liquidity insights to sales teams.
  • Monitor market developments, credit spreads, and macroeconomic indicators to identify opportunities.
  • Maintain and manage trading books in coordination with a senior trader, within established risk parameters.
  • Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal trading policies.
  • Develop and maintain relationships with counterparties, including dealers and institutional clients.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, or related field.
  • 5+ years of experience trading corporate bonds in a buy-side or sell-side environment.
  • Strong analytical skills and deep understanding of fixed income products.
  • Proven track record of generating trading ideas and managing risk effectively.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Proficiency in Bloomberg, Tradeweb, MarketAxess, and other trading platforms.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
  • Securities Industry Essentials Exam Series 7 and 63 licenses required or the ability to obtain within 60 days.

What we offer:

  • Competitive salary
  • Medical, Dental and Vision
  • Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
  • Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
  • Paid holidays
  • Professional Development Opportunities
  • Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
  • Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
  • Charitable gift-matching program
  • Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.

jobs.title: Senior Associate/Associate, Portfolio Management, Product and Investor Relations, Real Estate
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

We are looking to hire a Senior Associate/Associate to join our Real Estate Portfolio Management, Product and Investor Relations team in Hong Kong.

You will be working closely with Portfolio Management and Product & Investor Relations colleagues across the full lifecycle of fund management and capital raising activities. The role offers broad exposure to investment strategy, portfolio analysis, investor engagement, and new business initiatives, with responsibility for supporting ongoing fund operations, live capital raises, and prospective client and investor engagement across the platform.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

  • Support the Portfolio Management and Product & Investor Relations (IR) teams

  • Portfolio Management centered around strategy, investment review, portfolio analysis, internal and external reporting

    • Create and update asset and fund-level financial models

    • Perform in-depth portfolio analysis and reports

    • Conduct in-depth macroeconomic and sector research

    • New business development initiatives across all stages

  • Product and IR duties centered around the execution of live capital raises

    • Prepare marketing and investor due diligence materials (Investor Presentation, INREV DDQ, Private Placement Memorandum, etc.)

    • Assist investors with their queries and investment/operational/ESG due diligence questionnaires; organize and attend regular due diligence meetings and asset tours

    • Liaise and coordinate with third-party legal/tax/ESG consultants and advisors

    • Work with different teams and offices to review transaction documentation, negotiate key terms of the transaction with prospective investors, and close transactions

    • Support senior management with prospective client/investor meeting, including the preparation of investor profile, financial analysis, market assessment, recent transactions report, etc.); participate in prospective client/investor meetings

    • Maintain deal and investor profiles on our CRM system

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline

  • Prior experience in investment banking, private equity, and/or management consulting

  • Exceptional junior candidates at Analyst level, including recent graduates, may also be considered

  • Highly proficient with Microsoft Office (i.e., Word, Excel, PowerPoint) with excellent numeracy, analytical, presentation, and organizational skills

  • Self‑starter with a proactive mindset, able to think ahead, consistently meet deadlines, and anticipate next steps

  • Capacity to multitask, work independently across multiple projects and thrive in a team

  • Rigorous attention to detail and problem-solving skills

  • Intellectual curiosity and improvement mindset

  • Academic excellence, strong written and verbal communication skills, excellent interpersonal and relationship-building skills with colleagues and clients at all levels

  • High standards of professional and ethical conduct

  • Fluent English speaker with additional Japanese and/or Korean will be highly preferred

Reporting Relationships

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Head of Southeast Asia, Hong Kong and Australia, APAC Relationship Management
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

The Managing Director, Head of Southeast Asia, & Hong Kong ("Coverage Market"), APAC Relationship Management, is responsible for leading Ares’ client coverage, fundraising strategy, and long‑term relationship development across key Coverage Markets, with a particular focus on institutional investors including sovereign wealth funds, insurers, and pension systems. This role serves as a senior commercial leader for this Coverage Market, accountable for driving capital raising across the Ares platform, expanding strategic relationships, and representing Ares with the firm’s most significant and complex investors. The role includes leadership responsibility for regional Relationship Management professionals and close partnership with global investment, product, legal, compliance, and operations teams. The position operates with a high degree of autonomy and strategic influence and is a key contributor to Ares’ global fundraising objectives. The role reports to the head of APAC Relationship Management.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

Client Coverage & Fundraising

  • Lead institutional client coverage and fundraising activities across Southeast Asia, & Hong Kong, with responsibility for significant AUM growth across credit and alternative strategies

  • Originate, develop, and maintain senior‑level relationships with large institutional investors, including sovereign wealth funds, insurers, and pensions

  • Drive both new investor acquisition and expansion of existing client relationships across multiple strategies and mandates

  • Serve as a senior commercial representative of Ares in the region, engaging with clients on complex investment structures, bespoke mandates, and long‑term partnerships

Strategic Leadership

  • Shape and execute Ares’ APAC fundraising strategy in partnership with Global Client Solutions leadership

  • Identify regional market trends and growth opportunities to inform product strategy, investor targeting, and resource allocation

  • Act as a regional thought partner to investment teams on investor positioning, product development, and market demand

Team Leadership & Talent Development

  • Lead, manage, and develop a regional Relationship Management team, including hiring, mentoring, and performance management

  • Create a high‑performing, collaborative team culture aligned with Ares’ CREST values

  • Provide coaching and guidance to junior and mid‑level team members to build long‑term organizational capability

Cross‑Functional Collaboration

  • Partner closely with global Relationship Management, Product, Investment, Legal, Compliance, and Operations teams to deliver coordinated client solutions

  • Navigate internal stakeholder relationships to align firm priorities and deliver high‑quality client outcomes

  • Ensure consistency in client messaging, governance, and execution across regions and strategies

Risk Management & Governance

  • Exercise sound judgment in balancing growth opportunities with reputational, regulatory, and operational risk considerations

  • Ensure client engagements and fundraising activities adhere to internal governance standards and regulatory requirements

  • Serve as a senior escalation point for complex client or transactional matters in the region

Leadership & Influence

  • Acts as a visible culture carrier and senior leader for Ares in the APAC region

  • Represents the firm in high‑stakes client discussions, negotiations, and strategic forums

  • Operates collaboratively on matters of significant commercial and strategic importance while remaining aligned with firmwide objectives

Qualifications

  • Minimum of 15 years of experience in institutional relationship management, fundraising, or investor coverage within alternatives, credit, or asset management

  • Deep understanding of institutional investor and regional investment dynamics in Coverage Markets

  • Proven track record of driving significant capital formation and long‑term client growth

  • Demonstrated ability to lead, mentor, and scale high‑performing teams

  • Strong judgment, executive presence, and stakeholder management skills

Reporting Relationships

Partner, Head of APAC Relationship Management

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, U.S. Real Estate Logistics Asset Management
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

About Ares

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE: ARES) is a leading global alternative investment manager offering clients complementary primary and secondary investment solutions across the credit, real estate, private equity and infrastructure asset classes. We seek to provide flexible capital to support businesses and create value for our stakeholders and within our communities. By collaborating across our investment groups, we aim to generate consistent and attractive investment returns throughout market cycles. As of December 31, 2025, Ares Management's global platform had approximately $623 billion of assets under management with more than 4,200 employees operating across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East. For more information, please visit www.aresmgmt.com.

About Marq

Marq is a global leader in the development and operation of modern logistics facilities and manages a portfolio of approximately 2,000 properties totaling more than 600 million square feet. With a mission to deliver institutional-quality logistics facilities and a consistent, best-in-class experience to tenants around the world, Marq’s highly specialized and dedicated team provides a powerful combination of global scale with local experience. Learn more at https://www.marqlogistics.com/.

Essential Job Duties

The Senior Associate will work within the Asset Management Group and assist in operating and reporting on a growing regional portfolio. Specific Responsibilities include:

  • Acting as a key partner in the operation and management of regional industrial assets

  • Conduct thorough market research to identify trends, competitive properties, and potential opportunities for leasing

  • Negotiate lease terms and rental rates with potential tenants, ensuring favorable outcomes for both the company and the tenants

  • Oversight of third-party leasing teams, property management, vendor contracts and vendor relationships supporting the industrial assets

jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

About Ares

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE: ARES) is a leading global alternative investment manager offering clients complementary primary and secondary investment solutions across the credit, real estate, private equity and infrastructure asset classes. We seek to provide flexible capital to support businesses and create value for our stakeholders and within our communities. By collaborating across our investment groups, we aim to generate consistent and attractive investment returns throughout market cycles. As of December 31, 2025, Ares Management's global platform had approximately $623 billion of assets under management with more than 4,200 employees operating across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East. For more information, please visit www.aresmgmt.com.

About Marq

Marq is a global leader in the development and operation of modern logistics facilities and manages a portfolio of approximately 2,000 properties totaling more than 600 million square feet. With a mission to deliver institutional-quality logistics facilities and a consistent, best-in-class experience to tenants around the world, Marq’s highly specialized and dedicated team provides a powerful combination of global scale with local experience. Learn more at https://www.marqlogistics.com/.

Essential Job Duties

The Senior Associate will work within the Asset Management Group and assist in operating and reporting on a growing regional portfolio. Specific Responsibilities include:

  • Acting as a key partner in the operation and management of regional industrial assets.

  • Conduct thorough market research to identify trends, competitive properties, and potential opportunities for leasing.

  • Negotiate lease terms and rental rates with potential tenants, ensuring favorable outcomes for both the company and the tenants.

  • Oversight of third-party leasing teams, property management, vendor contracts and vendor relationships supporting the industrial assets.

  • Direct involvement and responsibilities for new and ongoing lease negotiations within the portfolio including communicating with third-party leasing teams, in-house counsel, and third-party legal teams.

  • Assist in administration of capital expenditures within the industrial portfolio and working with the VP Construction Management on oversight and guidance to all third-party construction managers, contractors and/or vendors.

  • Assist in the post-acquisition budget process, annual budget process and reforecasting and strategic planning for the entire regional industrial portfolio.  This includes overseeing the timeline, preparation and completion of annual budgets, business plans and leasing and marketing plans.

  • Assisting with due diligence efforts performed during the acquisition phase, specifically: tenant interviews, tenant estoppels, underwriting assumptions (market rent, lease up timing, leasing costs, and capital improvements) and third-party reports (environmental and engineering reports) for future acquisitions.

  • Responsible for accuracy and timeliness of management reporting on the portfolio: review of monthly third-party reports, verifying new leases are input correctly by internal property management teams, as well as assisting in the compilation of internal asset management portfolio reports.

  • Assist with tenant move-in, move-out and make-readies.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Position requires a bachelor’s degree. 

  • 5+ years direct experience in asset management is preferred. Leasing experience preferred.  

  • A team-oriented individual with strong verbal and written communication skills who is comfortable working in a very entrepreneurial environment required. 

  • Demonstrated ability to effectively manage transactional details required. 

  • Demonstrated budgeting skills, including Yardi and Excel applications are preferred.

  • Ability to effectively manage internal and external relationships required. 

  • Ability to travel domestically required

Reporting Relationships

Principal, U.S. Real Estate Logistics Asset Management

Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus. Note, the range takes into account a broad spectrum of qualifications, including, but not limited to, years of relevant work experience, education, and other relevant qualifications specific to the role.

$140,000.00 - $160,000.00

The firm also offers robust Benefits offerings. Ares U.S. Core Benefits include Comprehensive Medical/Rx, Dental and Vision plans; 401(k) program with company match; Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA); Healthcare Savings Accounts (HSA) with company contribution; Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance; Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD) insurance; Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and Commuter Benefits plan for parking and transit.

Ares offers a number of additional benefits including access to a world-class medical advisory team, a mental health app that includes coaching, therapy and psychiatry, a mindfulness and wellbeing app, financial wellness benefit that includes access to a financial advisor, new parent leave, reproductive and adoption assistance, emergency backup care, matching gift program, education sponsorship program, and much more.

jobs.title: AVP, Fund Accounting
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

Ares Management is seeking an Associate Vice President (“AVP”) for our US Public Credit accounting team. The AVP will be responsible for the day-to-day accounting and oversight of several controlled portfolio companies of publicly traded business development companies (“BDCs”).

Primary functions and essential responsibilities

  • Assist with preparation of US GAAP financial statements for monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, and annual reporting deadlines for the various funds.
  • Prepare quarterly GAAP consolidated financial statements for an SEC-registered investment advisor.
  • Oversee and review daily, monthly and quarterly NAV packages from the fund administrator.
  • Assist with monthly/quarterly close process and preparation of various financial statement account balance reconciliations and workpapers.
  • Work closely with operations and other groups in managing daily cash and position breaks
  • Manage daily cash activities for several funds, including processing of payments, approving and coding invoices and posting to the general ledger.
  • Assist in managing relationships and liaising with internal resources, fund administers and auditors to meet all deadlines.
  • High level of interaction across the organization, including communications with portfolio managers, investor relations, legal, compliance, operations, tax, FP&A and financial reporting.
  • Provide the BDCs and management company with data for quarterly SEC filings.
  • Prepare quarterly waterfall calculations and review monthly distribution reports from the trustee.
  • Provide and review detailed reporting to investors and internal constituents (portfolio managers, investor relations)
  • Provide compliance reporting (Form PF, ADV, etc.)
  • Participate and contribute to regular review of processes and deliverables to improve effectiveness, efficiency, accuracy to ensure scalability as the firm continues to grow rapidly.
  • Various ad-hoc/special projects.

Qualifications

Education:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

Certification:

  • CPA (preferred)

Experience Required:  

  • Minimum 6-9 years of experience in fund accounting preferred
  • Public accounting experience, Big 4 (preferred)
  • Industry experience, specifically with bank debt preferred
  • Experience with Advent Geneva, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central or Onestream software a plus

General Requirements:

  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, high growth environment
  • Highly motivated team player with a strong work ethic
  • Strong understanding of US GAAP
  • Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial instruments, and regulatory requirements
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
  • Strong verbal and written skills; ability to analyze and summarize complex information both verbally and in writing
  • Ability to multi-task and manage various deadlines
  • Excellent organizational and interpersonal skills
  • Intermediate or advanced Excel user (e.g. complex formulas, macros)
  • PowerBI, Alteryx, Anaplan or other business intelligence tools a plus

Reporting Relationships

Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus. Note, the range takes into account a broad spectrum of qualifications, including, but not limited to, years of relevant work experience, education, and other relevant qualifications specific to the role.

$150,000 - $180,000

The firm also offers robust Benefits offerings. Ares U.S. Core Benefits include Comprehensive Medical/Rx, Dental and Vision plans; 401(k) program with company match; Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA); Healthcare Savings Accounts (HSA) with company contribution; Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance; Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD) insurance; Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and Commuter Benefits plan for parking and transit.

Ares offers a number of additional benefits including access to a world-class medical advisory team, a mental health app that includes coaching, therapy and psychiatry, a mindfulness and wellbeing app, financial wellness benefit that includes access to a financial advisor, new parent leave, reproductive and adoption assistance, emergency backup care, matching gift program, education sponsorship program, and much more.

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.description:

Job Description

Ares is currently searching for a highly motivated, detail-oriented team player to join as an Associate on the Alternative Credit (“Alt Credit”) investment team in the London office, focused on Asset Management.

The position will be responsible for monitoring and on-going surveillance of Alternative Credit portfolio investments, developing and running complex discounted cash flow models, monitoring and reporting on investment performance metrics and portfolio-level metrics, providing assistance to deal teams with assigned investments and general asset management including fundings, settlements, valuation and reporting.

The nature of the team’s investment process and dynamic environment necessitates that a candidate have strong verbal and written communication skills, attention to detail, a professional demeanour, along with sharp analytical skills relevant to structured investments.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

  • Monitor portfolio of private investments, including monitor covenants, review and analyze underlying asset performance and financial information

  • General asset management including executing fundings, processing monthly settlements, performing mark-to-market valuations, and other asset-level and portfolio-level reporting

  • Develop and run complex models for valuation and reporting purposes

  • Provide support to rest of the deal team with assigned investments

  • Frequent communication with portfolio companies

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree required or international equivalent in Economics, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, Finance or related field

  • Strong intellect with solid communication, quantitative, financial and analytical skills

  • 2+ year of finance or accounting experience with exposure to structured finance/securitization preferred

General Requirements

  • Advanced MS Excel modeling skills (index + match, macros, and array formulas)

  • Intermediate MS PowerPoint skills

  • Self-motivated and driven; ability to work effectively in a collegial, fast moving, demanding, and dynamic environment

  • Creative problem solver

  • Exceptional interpersonal skills

  • Impeccable integrity and trustworthiness

Reporting Relationships

jobs.title: Senior Associate/Vice President, Capital Solutions (Bank Coverage)
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

The Global Capital Solutions team focuses on liabilities sourcing and financing, as well as asset sourcing and strategic counterparty relationships across the Ares platform.

The Senior Associate or Vice President will play a central role in building and maintaining the firm’s counterparty reporting and coverage infrastructure. The role is focused on developing systems, analytics, and standardized materials that enable senior leadership to evaluate bank relationships, allocate wallet share, and engage with key counterparties using consistent, high‑quality information. 

This position sits within Ares’ growing Capital Solutions business and represents the ability to have high impact into determining and building how counterparty data is captured, organized, and presented across the platform, working closely with investment teams, the technology team, and senior stakeholders.

Role Overview 

  • Establish and maintain a centralized, analytics‑driven view of bank activity and value‑add across the firm. The role is responsible for evolving an existing static scorecard into a more dynamic, repeatable reporting tool, including development of counterparty dashboards and integration into CRM systems to support firm‑wide visibility. 
  • Support strategic decision‑making through structured reporting and analysis. 
  • Consolidate platform‑wide interactions with banks—deal activity, fees, coverage touchpoints, and qualitative inputs—into clear, standardized outputs used to inform wallet allocation discussions, negotiation preparation, and senior‑level reviews. 
  • Advance a unified counterparty coverage and information model. 
  • Support development of a centralized resource for counterparty intelligence, providing leadership with timely insight into fees, interactions, opportunities, and priorities across the organization. 
  • Produce executive‑level relationship materials. 
  • Synthesize scorecard outputs, value‑received metrics, and platform‑wide data into materials used for annual bank meetings, CEO‑level discussions, and other strategic counterparty engagements, incorporating input from investment teams as needed. 

Responsibilities 

  • Develop and maintain analytical frameworks to quantify bank value‑add, including deals sourced, capital raised, and breadth of engagement across the platform. 
  • Generate bank‑level scorecards, dashboards, and comparative analyses using standardized fee and activity data. 
  • Prepare briefing materials and reporting packages used in wallet allocation discussions and senior counterparty meetings. 
  • Coordinate with Capital Solutions and investment teams to collect and incorporate forward‑looking priorities into counterparty materials. 
  • Partner closely with the technology team to translate reporting and analytics needs into system enhancements and ensure effective implementation. 
  • Support the adoption and consistent use of CRM and related systems by improving data quality, reporting usability, and standardization. 
  • Build and maintain templated executive‑level materials (scorecards, dashboards, relationship summaries) that can be refreshed systematically over time. 

Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in capital markets, investor relations, treasury, DCM, or a strategy / COO / FP&A role within an asset manager or bank. 
  • Strong analytical and data skills, including experience with Excel and data visualization tools (Power BI / Tableau); familiarity with CRM systems such as DealCloud or Salesforce. 
  • Knowledge of alternative investments and lending a plus.
  • Detail‑oriented and process‑driven, with the ability to manage complex datasets and produce consistent, high‑quality reporting. 
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, particularly in preparing materials for senior stakeholders. 
  • Systems‑minded and execution‑focused; comfortable driving standardization and reducing ad‑hoc or off‑system processes. 
  • Commercially aware, with an understanding of how reporting supports wallet allocation, negotiations, and relationship management.

Reporting Relationships

Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus. Note, the range takes into account a broad spectrum of qualifications, including, but not limited to, years of relevant work experience, education, and other relevant qualifications specific to the role.

$140,000.00 - $180,000.00

The firm also offers robust Benefits offerings. Ares U.S. Core Benefits include Comprehensive Medical/Rx, Dental and Vision plans; 401(k) program with company match; Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA); Healthcare Savings Accounts (HSA) with company contribution; Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance; Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD) insurance; Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and Commuter Benefits plan for parking and transit.

Ares offers a number of additional benefits including access to a world-class medical advisory team, a mental health app that includes coaching, therapy and psychiatry, a mindfulness and wellbeing app, financial wellness benefit that includes access to a financial advisor, new parent leave, reproductive and adoption assistance, emergency backup care, matching gift program, education sponsorship program, and much more.

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Investment Analyst, Asia Credit
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

Ares Asia Credit team is looking to hire an Investment Analyst to join its Investment team based in Shanghai. As part of the investment team, the individual will help to make direct special situations and credit investments. The individual will perform in-depth analysis on potential investment opportunities and build up a detailed understanding of the industry, the competitive dynamics therein and the target company.


The work will involve both desktop research, such as analysis and modelling of financial accounts, as well as on-the-ground work. While the work will be primarily based in Shanghai, the role will have a pan-Asia scope and may require the candidate to travel within Asia. The individual will also assist in deal execution, the ongoing management and exit of investments.


We are looking for self-motivated individuals with a strong interest in investing and a desire to gain exposure to a wide variety of industries and transaction structures. Individuals comfortable in a fast-paced environment will be best suited for the role.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

  • Work directly with senior investment professionals on new and existing deals

  • Interact with investee companies and other industry participants to manage investment positions and gather industry insights

  • Research, compile industry data and understand industry trends and competitive dynamics

  • Collect company level operational and financial data

  • Analyze financial statements

  • Build and update detailed financial models for companies

  • Perform valuation using different methodologies

  • Identify potential investment risks and consider potential mitigants

  • Distill the insights of analysis and present investment recommendations

  • Prepare Investment Committee (IC) memorandums

  • Participate in meetings with investee companies and with the in-house investment committee

  • Handle other ad-hoc projects

Qualifications

  • Minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics, Mathematics or Statistics

  • Strong academic performance (minimum GPA of 4.1/5.0 or 3.55/4.3 or 3.3/4.0)

  • Minimum of 2 years of experience in investment banking, corporate credit analysis, private credit/direct lending, special situations investing, private equity, or transaction advisory

  • Passion for investing and valuation

  • Self-motivated, enjoy challenges and willing to continually learn

  • Strong analytical skills and proficiency with numbers

  • Advanced MS Excel and financial modelling skills

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills in English, and Chinese preferred

  • Excellent editorial and review skills

  • Initiative and entrepreneurial spirit

  • Ability to explain facts and findings in a clear and succinct manner (both verbally and in writing)

  • Possess multi-tasking skills and ability to work well under pressure

  • Strong team player

Reporting Relationships

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Vice President or Principal, Commercial Finance, Originations
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

Ares is currently seeking a candidate for the position of Vice President or Principal to join our originations team on the Ares Commercial Finance (“ACF”) Group in the Northeast.  This person will cover the client base based in the Northeast.

ACF leverages the scale and flexible capital of the Ares platform to provide innovative asset-based and cash flow solutions to small and middle-market companies, as well as asset-backed facilities to specialty finance companies. Our senior ACF investment professionals have on average over 30 years of experience in commercial finance and asset-based lending.

Our team uses a combination of specialized credit and collateral monitoring to provide clients with flexibility and greater credit availability. ACF has the ability to structure and agent deals to support the growth and changing capital needs of its clients. Our asset-based lines of credit may be structured as working capital financing, bridge financing, turnaround financing, acquisition financing, debtor-in-possession financing and special accommodation financing.

The following highlights ACF’s Direct Lending strategy:

  • Leverage the power of the Ares and Direct Lending platforms
  • Multi-asset class “one-stop” solutions to meet clients’ needs
  • Ability to rotate between asset classes with superior relative value
  • Drive proprietary investment opportunities by pursuing multiple origination channels across various regions within the United States
  • Active management of risk through structuring and asset coverage
  • Agent transactions with careful monitoring of liquidity and performance
  • Small deal teams with single point of contact
  • Transparent and streamlined approval process with certainty of closing

Primary functions and essential responsibilities:

  • Utilize existing network and networking skills to originate viable new business opportunities
  • Work on new opportunities generated by the originator or other origins of the opportunity. 
  • Serve as liaison between Prospect and the Legal Department to process Non-Disclosure Agreements.
  • Request prospect credit package to determine if the opportunity merits formal review.
  • Analyze and structure new business opportunities for credit approval by assisting in preparing of pre-clear memo for credit committee review and approval.
  • Work with the prospect and Underwriter to determine any up front concerns for clarification prior to initial credit analysis/presentation of pre-clear memo.
  • Assist in the preparing of initial credit analysis/pre-clear memo for approval; creation of term sheet by Underwriter.
  • Present term sheets to prospects for approval; negotiate terms with the goal being to move the opportunity into formal due diligence.
  • Work in conjunction with the Underwriter throughout the documentation process as needed.
  • Travel to prospect locations as needed.
  • Attendance at industry events to source business and further industry education as needed.
  • Participate in conference calls and meetings with Senior and Executive Management as needed.
  • Respond to prospect requests in a timely manner, whether via email or phone.
  • Prepare and submit weekly pipeline reports for review and discussion throughout the organization.
  • Keep current on industry trends.
  • Comply with established policies and procedures.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree or international equivalent required

  • Advanced knowledge of Asset Based Lending and commercial financial services practices and procedures
  • Ability to communicate effectively verbally and in writing
  • Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with employees, clients and outside contacts
  • Experience with asset backed lending, cash flow lending, direct lending etc. a plus
  • Strong structuring experience preferred

Reporting Relationships

Note, the range takes into account a broad spectrum of qualifications, including, but not limited to, years of relevant work experience, education, and other relevant qualifications specific to the role.

$140,000.00 - $200,000.00

The firm also offers robust Benefits offerings.
