jobs.title: Corporate Development Manager
jobs.description:
Company Overview

We are the better way to work in finance. As private equity’s value creation partner, we sit at the heart of PE—where sponsors and CFOs meet. Through financial consulting rooted in data, technology, and AI, we help clients drive value—supporting the office of the CFO to drive end-to-end value creation.

If you crave challenging work and are looking to grow, come solve complex issues alongside 1,600+ finance & technology experts in a supportive, collaborative environment. 

Backed by premier private equity firms and headquartered in New York with 11 offices around the globe, we are a high-growth, entrepreneurial firm looking for people who want to be part of building something great. Come make your mark. 

Position Overview

The Corporate Development Manager will own end-to-end inorganic growth initiatives — from sourcing and evaluating opportunities to negotiating terms and assisting with post-close integration — to advance our strategic roadmap and value creation objectives. This new hire will partner with the Head of M&A to:

  • Drive the full M&A lifecycle (build pipeline, diligence, valuation, deal terms, close, and integrate)
  • Translate investment theses into actionable business cases with quantified synergies, return profiles, and execution plans 

This role will be based in our NYC office and is a hybrid role with the flexibility to work remotely 2 days a week, blending the best of both worlds for efficiency and collaboration. Ideal candidates should be local to the desired location (NYC).

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.

What You’ll Do:

  • Pipeline and Sourcing – Build and maintain a prioritized funnel of targets aligned to strategic themes; cultivate relationships with founders, bankers, and investors; triage inbound opportunities with crisp fit/no-fit rationale
  • Deal Evaluation – Lead commercial, financial, and operational assessments; structure scenarios, sensitivities, and synergy cases; summarize go/no-go recommendations for executive stakeholders
  • Financial Modeling and Valuation – Build robust three-statement, DCF, trading/transaction comps, and LBO-style downside cases; frame offer ranges and walk-away points; align structure with risk-adjusted returns
  • Diligence Leadership – Run cross-functional diligence workstreams (legal, finance, tax, HR, operations); own the data room, Q&A tracking, red flag resolution, and IC-ready findings
  • Deal Structuring and Negotiation – Draft and iterate key documents (NDAs, LOIs, term sheets, purchase agreements)
  • Integration Planning and Oversight – Serve as DRI for Day-1/100, value-capture workplans, and KPI tracking; ensure explicit owners, timelines, and interlocks across functions; course-correct against synergy drift
  • Executive Communications and Governance – Prepare investment memos, IC materials, board updates, and post-close readouts; establish deal KPIs and cadence for performance management
  • Market and Competitive Intelligence – Maintain a live view of sector dynamics, adjacencies, and disruptors; refresh build/partner/buy perspectives and target lists accordingly

You Have:

  • Minimum 3–6 years in corporate development, investment banking, and/or private equity with demonstrated end-to-end exposure to M&A processes (modeling, diligence, negotiation, and closing)
  • Expert proficiency in deal execution and company evaluation; strong command of transaction structures
  • Strong document familiarity (NDAs, LOIs, SPAs), term mechanics (earnouts, RWI, escrows), and data room best practices
  • High-impact communication skills (concise memos, IC/board materials, stakeholder alignment) and executive presence
  • Industry competence in tech-enabled services, professional services, or human capital services preferred 

You Are:

  • Excited to be part of a growing team, with a focus on driving future growth
  • Passionate about delivering exceptional client service
  • Knowledgeable about the relationship between Private Equity sponsors and their portfolio companies, particularly within the office of the CFO
  • Willing to get your hands dirty in the details of a project while simultaneously seeing the whole picture
  • Comfortable managing projects with multiple complex workstreams while also focused on your single workstream (you are managing and doing)
  • A self-starter with a strong work ethic
  • Full of entrepreneurial spirit and comfortable in a fluid, flat organization
  • Able to effectively communicate complex issues and solutions, and raise issues to senior team members when necessary
  • A team player, able to work with team members across all levels
  • Someone who enjoys mentoring others and doing meaningful work
  • A leader of others; you lead by example

The annual salary for this role ranges from: $160,000 to $200,000 USD + benefits + bonus. Actual compensation packages are determined by evaluating a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to geographic location, skill set, years and depth of experience, education, certifications, cost of labor and internal equity.

We are committed to building a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills.  We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Portfolio
jobs.description:
Acumen Latin America is seeking a Senior Associate to join our Bogotá-based Portfolio team. We are looking for a thoughtful, entrepreneurial, and mission-driven professional with 4–6 years of relevant experience and a strong commitment to tackling poverty and inequality in Latin America.

This role is ideal for someone who combines strong investment judgment with empathy, curiosity, and the ability to work closely with entrepreneurs and rural communities. The right candidate is analytical, proactive, open to different points of view, and energized by finding creative solutions in complex and evolving environments.

About Acumen

Acumen is a global force of entrepreneurs, investors, philanthropists, and social innovators working together to build a world based on dignity. We were founded by Jacqueline Novogratz on the radical idea that business, when cultivated with moral imagination, can break the cycle of poverty. We invest in transformational companies, build sustainable markets, and prepare leaders with the tools they need to create a more just and inclusive future. Since 2001, we have scaled companies and shaped markets in some of the hardest-to-reach communities on the planet, impacting over half-a-billion lives. To learn more visit acumen.org.

About Acumen Latin America

Acumen began investing in Latin America in 2013, with a focus on Colombia and Peru. Our strategy is centered on backing businesses that serve communities that have faced historical political, economic, and social exclusion, particularly in rural areas. Our Bogotá-based team invests in and supports companies across sectors such as agriculture, education, energy, and other high-impact areas aligned with Acumen’s investment thesis.

About the Role

The Senior Associate will play a key role across the full investment lifecycle: sourcing and evaluating new opportunities, conducting due diligence, structuring transactions, supporting investment committee processes, coordinating disbursements, and actively managing portfolio companies post-investment support.

This person will help assess opportunities from the ground up, including businesses that are not yet in the pipeline, and will work from initial screening through investment decision and closing. The role also requires close partnership with portfolio companies, ranging from support on operational matters to participation in board meetings and strategic discussions.

Responsibilities

Early-Stage Investing

  • Identify, assess, and help build a high-quality pipeline of investment opportunities aligned with Acumen’s investment thesis, including evaluating opportunities from zero through investment decision and disbursement. 
  • Conduct rigorous commercial, operational, financial, and impact due diligence on potential investments. 
  • Build financial models, valuation analysis, and contribute to transaction structuring. 
  • Prepare clear, compelling investment materials and recommendations for Acumen Latin America’s Investment Committee. 
  • Work directly with entrepreneurs, rural enterprises, and community-based businesses to understand business models, risks, opportunities, and impact potential. 

Portfolio Management

  • Serve as a thought partner to portfolio companies, providing support that may range from hands-on operational problem-solving to strategic guidance. 
  • Build trusted relationships with entrepreneurs and management teams, bringing empathy, sound judgment, and a practical approach to problem solving. 
  • Help identify key company needs and design support strategies in coordination with senior team members and external partners. 
  • Track financial and impact performance and help companies strengthen their ability to scale sustainably. 

Portfolio Operations & Team Contribution

  • Support internal portfolio analysis, investment financial and impact performance tracking, and documentation of key insights and lessons learned. 
  • Contribute to the development of materials for strategic partnerships, fundraising, and external communications as needed. 
  • Help strengthen Acumen’s network across the impact investing and social enterprise ecosystem. 
  • Contribute to a collaborative team culture by listening to diverse perspectives, challenging assumptions thoughtfully, and helping the team arrive at strong, creative solutions. 

Qualifications

  • 4–6 years of relevant experience in venture capital, private equity, investment banking, consulting, impact investing, or related fields. 
  • Strong financial, analytical, and business judgment, including experience evaluating early-stage or growth-stage businesses. 
  • Experience conducting due diligence, financial modelling and supporting transaction execution. 
  • Ability to operate with independence, attention to detail, and strong execution in fast-paced and sometimes ambiguous environments. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills and an authentic ability to build trust with entrepreneurs, colleagues, and external stakeholders. 
  • Demonstrated interest in working with underserved communities, particularly in rural contexts. 
  • Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree or MBA is a plus. 
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Spanish. 
  • Authorization to work in Colombia on a permanent basis. 

What We’re Looking For

  • Deep alignment with Acumen’s mission and values. 
  • A genuine desire to listen and understand different perspectives. 
  • Empathy, humility, and strong relationship-building skills. 
  • Creativity and the ability to think outside the box when facing challenges. 
  • Sound judgment, initiative, and a bias toward action. 
  • Curiosity, maturity, and a commitment to learning.

Compensation
Acumen Latin America offers a competitive compensation package commensurate with experience, including benefits.

Location
Bogotá, Colombia 

Deadline
Candidates are encouraged to apply early, as applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

jobs.title: Class of 2026 Full Time Analyst
jobs.description:
Firm Overview

GLC Advisors & Co. is a leading independent advisory firm providing objective, conflict-free advice on complex strategic and financial transactions. The firm operates under a senior-led execution model, partnering closely with boards of directors, management teams, and financial sponsors on critical corporate decisions.
GLC’s partners and senior bankers collectively have advised on 900+ completed transactions totaling more than $800 billion in aggregate transaction value, with prior experience at the world’s most selective bulge-bracket and elite boutique investment banks. The firm is consistently engaged on high-impact, analytically intensive mandates across mergers and acquisitions, capital structure advisory, restructurings, and valuation matters.
GLC Advisors maintains offices in New York, Denver, Los Angeles, and San Francisco.

Role Overview

Our Los Angeles office is seeking to recruit talented individuals to join with our 2026 Full-Time Analyst class (starting summer 2026). Analysts provide direct support to senior bankers and are expected to handle a high level of responsibility in the execution of restructuring and distressed financing transactions.
The Los Angeles office provides a combination of top-tier restructuring talent and a lean, entrepreneurial team environment. Analysts work on small deal teams in a tight-knit, collegial culture that emphasizes mentorship, apprenticeship, and long-term development. For candidates seeking the standards and training of a leading restructuring platform without the layers of a large analyst pool, this role offers uncommon exposure, accelerated development, and strong potential for increased responsibility over time.

Responsibilities

Analysts will assume significant ownership across all phases of transaction execution, including:

  • Analyzing companies, industries, capital structures, liquidity profiles, historical financial statements, and projected financial information
  • Building financial models and performing valuation analyses

Investment Banking Associate

at GLC Advisors & Co., LLC (View all jobs)

Firm Overview

GLC Advisors & Co. is a leading independent advisory firm providing objective, conflict-free advice on complex strategic and financial transactions. The firm operates under a senior-led execution model, partnering closely with boards of directors, management teams, and financial sponsors on critical corporate decisions.

GLC’s partners and senior bankers collectively have advised on 900+ completed transactions totaling more than $800 billion in aggregate transaction value, with prior experience at the world’s most selective bulge-bracket and elite boutique investment banks. The firm is consistently engaged on high-impact, analytically intensive mandates across mergers and acquisitions, capital structure advisory, restructurings, and valuation matters.

GLC Advisors maintains offices in New York, Denver, Los Angeles, and San Francisco.

Role Overview 

GLC Advisors is seeking a top-tier Investment Banking Associate to join its Gaming advisory franchise in Denver. This position is intended for candidates with exceptional technical ability, sound judgment, and a demonstrated interest in executing complex transactions in a lean, performance-driven environment.

The Gaming practice advises across the full interactive entertainment and gaming ecosystem, including gaming technology and software, payments and infrastructure, land-based gaming and entertainment, iGaming, sports betting, and lodging. The team regularly serves as a trusted advisor to industry leaders, growth-oriented companies, and sophisticated financial sponsors on high-profile strategic and financial transactions.

Associates are expected to function as core execution leaders on live mandates, working directly with senior bankers and clients. The role offers substantial responsibility, early client exposure, and meaningful influence over transaction outcomes.

Responsibilities

Associates will assume significant ownership across all phases of transaction execution, including:

  • Leading the analytical and execution workstreams for M&A, financing, and restructuring assignments
  • Working directly with clients, counterparties, and legal and accounting advisors to advance transactions from mandate through closing
  • Overseeing and mentoring Analysts while ensuring the highest standards of analytical rigor and presentation quality
  • Developing detailed financial models, valuation analyses, and transaction materials used in board-level and investor discussions
  • Conducting in-depth industry, company, and strategic analyses to support advisory recommendations
  • Participating in management presentations, due diligence processes, and negotiation discussions

Qualifications

GLC Advisors seeks candidates with a demonstrated record of excellence, including:

  • Outstanding analytical capability, intellectual curiosity, and professional judgment
  • Prior investment banking experience as an Analyst or Associate strongly preferred
  • 2–4 years of relevant transaction experience in M&A, capital markets, or restructuring
  • Advanced financial modeling, valuation, and accounting proficiency
  • Ability to independently manage complex workstreams in a fast-paced, high-expectation environment
  • Strong leadership skills and experience managing junior team members
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
  • High level of attention to detail and commitment to quality
  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • FINRA Series 79 or Series 7 strongly preferred (required within 90 days of start date)

Compensation & Culture

GLC Advisors offers highly competitive compensation, including performance-based bonus opportunities, commensurate with experience and contribution. The firm provides a comprehensive benefits package and fosters a collegial, intellectually rigorous, and entrepreneurial culture, with a strong emphasis on accountability, professional development, and excellence in execution.

Equal Opportunity Employer

GLC Advisors is an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable law. The firm complies with all requirements related to reasonable accommodation.

jobs.title: Investment Banking Intern
jobs.description:
SUMMER INTERN, INVESTMENT BANKING

COMPANY: GLC Advisors & Co., LLC

LOCATION: Denver, CO

POSITION TITLE: Investment Banking Summer Intern 

EMPLOYMENT TYPE: Internship

PREFERRED START DATE: June 2026

FIRM OVERVIEW:

GLC is a leading independent investment banking firm that delivers objective, senior- level advice within a number of industries including: business services, consumer & retail, energy & power, financial services, gaming, general industrials, healthcare, media & telecom, metals & mining and technology.

GLC was founded in 2009 and brings financial advisory experience from both Wall Street and specialized boutique investment banking firms. With over 60 professionals, we have worked through multiple economic cycles dating back to the 1980’s and have led more than 900 transactions representing over $800 billion in total transaction value.

Our comprehensive service offerings involve all aspects of sell-side and buy-side M&A, capital raising and restructuring.

Our offices are in New York, Denver, Los Angeles and San Francisco. For more information visit www.glca.com.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Individuals will be assigned a wide variety of projects and given as much responsibility as their experience and capabilities permit. Responsibilities include:

  • Building financial models and performing valuation analyses
  • Preparing presentations, company offering memorandums, management presentations, and other marketing materials
  • Analyzing company historical financial statements and prospective financial information
  • Conducting extensive industry and company research
  • Collaborating within M&A execution teams

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Current undergraduate or graduate student with an expected graduation date in Summer 2027
  • Prior experience in corporate finance, accounting, private equity, investment banking, or related financial services
  • Pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration or Management, Economics, or other degree from an accredited university or college
  • Proficient in Excel and PowerPoint, as well as preferred experience with other financial information tools such as S&P Capital IQ and PitchBook
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills with a high level of attention to detail
  • Outstanding work ethic with ability to perform in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong desire to develop modeling and financial statement analysis skills
  • Ability and willingness to work hard beyond typical work week hours

OFFERING:

We offer an exciting work environment with an entrepreneurial spirit. This position offers the opportunity to gain in-depth and diverse work experience in investment banking.

Please include in your cover letter why you are interested in our Denver team and Denver, CO more broadly.

Firm Overview

GLC Advisors & Co. is a leading independent advisory firm providing objective, conflict-free advice on complex strategic and financial transactions. The firm operates under a senior-led execution model, partnering closely with boards of directors, management teams, and financial sponsors on critical corporate decisions.

GLC has advised on landmark transactions including Hertz, iHeartMedia, Puerto Rico, and Toys “R” Us, among many others. The firm’s senior professionals collectively have advised on 900+ completed transactions totaling more than $800 billion in aggregate transaction value, with prior experience at the world’s most selective bulge-bracket and elite boutique investment banks. The firm is consistently engaged on high-impact, analytically intensive mandates across mergers and acquisitions, capital structure advisory, restructurings, and valuation matters.

GLC Advisors maintains offices in New York, Denver, Los Angeles, and San Francisco.

Role Overview

Lead end-to-end execution of equity and debt capital markets transactions.

Advise clients on market positioning, capital structure optimization, and transaction structuring, delivering differentiated insight tailored to strategic, opportunistic, and event-driven objectives.

Serve as a senior point of contact throughout active mandates and broader relationship management initiatives.

Partner closely with sector and coverage teams to originate capital markets assignments and develop integrated financing solutions.

Focus primarily on private capital raising transactions (new money private debt / equity, staple financings, and other structured transactions).

Responsibilities

  • Direct the preparation of offering memoranda, investor presentations, internal credit materials, valuation analyses, and transaction documentation. Manage the full execution lifecycle — including diligence coordination, financial modeling, documentation review, regulatory interface, investor marketing, bookbuilding, pricing, and closing.

  • Utilize institutional data platforms and analytics tools and advanced Microsoft Office applications, to inform transaction strategy and investor targeting.
  • Mentor and develop junior professionals, instilling rigorous analytical standards, disciplined execution processes, and a client-first mindset.

Qualifications

GLC Advisors seeks candidates with a demonstrated record of excellence, including:

  • Prior experience as a Vice President within a Capital Markets Group required
  • Existing relationships with (i) private equity sponsor clients and (ii) private debt / equity capital sources
  • Exceptional analytical capability, intellectual curiosity, and professional judgement
  • Demonstrated leadership and team management experience
  • Advanced financial modeling, valuation, and financial statement analysis skills
  • Ability to independently manage complex workstreams in high-pressure, time-sensitive environments
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • High level of attention to detail and commitment to quality
  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • FINRA Series 79 or Series 7 strongly preferred (required within 90 days of start date)

GLC Advisors offers highly competitive compensation, including performance-based bonus opportunities, commensurate with experience and contribution. jobs.title: Associate, Capital Advisory
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in our offices Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays.

Lincoln International is seeking an Associate to join our growing Capital Advisory Team, based in Frankfurt. Successful candidates have excellent cognitive and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills, and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading M&A advisory firm. Successful Associates are motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, people oriented and entrepreneurial environment.

In Germany, our team comprises over 90 bankers covering a very broad range of sectors, including Industrials & Renewables, Consumer, Technology, Business Services, Healthcare, as well as Capital Advisory, located in Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. We are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell sides for private equity clients. In 2023, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 300 transactions worldwide.

The European Lincoln International Capital Advisory Group successfully advised on 350+ transactions worldwide since its inception in the range of Euro 50 to 350 million. In 2024 we had another record performance of our global Capital Advisory Group by providing creative and alternative financing solutions to clients out of 5 European offices with more than 25 dedicated professionals in order to secure capital for acquisition financings, investments, refinancings & recapitalisations, staple financings staving off defaults, restructuring businesses and navigating distressed situations and bankruptcies, and developing structured capital solutions.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform.

  • Conduct market research and financial analysis to support client engagements
  • Support senior team members within the Capital Advisory Group (CAG) in Frankfurt in executing debt financing transactions (acquisition financing, refinancing and recapitalisation deals) as part of the wider European CAG team
  • Prepare marketing documents and pitches
  • Carry out financial analysis & modelling, including analysing business forecasts, scenario analysis and covenant sensitivities
  • Identify and contacting finance providers on transactions
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams both across Europe within the CAG team as well as local M&A teams to ensure seamless project execution
  • Attend client meetings and participate in discussions
  • Interact with senior bankers and clients’ senior executives
  • Leading or implementing initiatives focused on growing the firm’s franchise value

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Associate to develop as quickly as he/she is capable.

The following factors differentiate the offering at Lincoln International:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, working in small deal teams with extensive client exposure as well as cross border contexts
  • Introduction to a vast array of industries
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant team exposure, including to very seasoned and experienced senior bankers and provides an opportunity to make a valued contribution and have an impact
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation and strategic thinking
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels
  • Opportunity to participate in our US and European Secondment Programmes
  • Mentoring Scheme, onboarding sessions and structured developmental feedback
  • The chance to build your career within a growth environment.  

Qualifications:

The Associate position at Lincoln International offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should bring the following requirements:

  • 3-4 years prior professional experience in Capital Advisory, Leveraged Finance, Transaction Services or Business Modelling experience, preferably in the mid market
  • Fluency and thorough understanding in Accounting, Corporate Finance and/or Leveraged Finance coupled with excellent Business Acumen
  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background, ideally educated to Masters level in a business related subject
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn
  • Solid and growing understanding of business models and sectors coupled with a good grasp of corporate strategy and themes related to company growth and development
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal growth, underpinned through professional and academic excellence as well as other pursuits and personal projects
  • Adaptability, dependability and the ability to manage a dynamic workload
  • Clear and engaging communication skills- verbally as well as written. Is able to relate and connect to other stakeholders quickly- clients, management teams and LI colleagues
  • Collaborative style and a personally engaging manner
  • International experiences and perspectives
  • Excellent written and spoken German and English skills, other European Languages are a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Click hereto view Lincoln International's Candidate Privacy Notices.

Lincoln International is seeking an Associate to join our European Healthcare team in London. Successful candidates will bring strong execution capabilities, sound judgement and the motivation to build a leading healthcare M&A advisory franchise in Europe. The role offers exposure across healthcare services, pharma services and the broader healthcare value chain, working closely with senior bankers and clients on both domestic and cross-border transactions.

We are looking for AI-forward individuals who are comfortable using AI tools in a professional environment. This includes effective prompting and developing practical agentic workflows to improve research, drafting, analysis and process execution, while applying rigorous quality control, human judgement and appropriate confidentiality standards.

Duties & Responsibilities:

This position provides an excellent opportunity to work in a demanding, yet supportive, team-oriented environment. An Associate at Lincoln International is expected to support both origination and execution, operate confidently across multiple workstreams and increasingly take ownership of core analytical and process tasks. The Associate also plays an important role in supervising Analysts, reviewing work product and helping maintain the quality and pace of client delivery.

  • Support the origination and execution of transactions, typically working on multiple projects and reporting to a Vice President, Director or Managing Director.
  • Oversee the work of Analysts, providing guidance and training and acting as the first reviewer of their work.
  • Lead the collection and analysis of complex datasets, including customer and operating data, to provide insights.
  • Draft information memoranda and other marketing materials, with a particular focus on financial and commercial evidence.
  • Design, build and finalize integrated operating models using best-practice standards and close collaboration with management teams.
  • Assist in the preparation of client presentations and other business development materials.
  • Assist in the valuation analysis of public and private companies and other analytical workstreams.
  • Take responsibility for drafting the EV to Equity bridge and deal funds flow under the guidance of the senior team.
  • Support due diligence, data room management, Q&A workstreams and interaction with clients, counterparties and third-party advisers.
  • Use AI tools, prompting techniques and practical agentic workflows responsibly to enhance research, drafting, data analysis and execution efficiency, while maintaining accuracy, confidentiality and appropriate human oversight.

Why Lincoln:

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Associate to develop as quickly as they are capable. The following factors differentiate the Associate program at Lincoln International:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this.
  • A true mid-cap approach, working in small deal teams with extensive client exposure as well as cross-border contexts.
  • Exposure to a broad range of healthcare subsectors, with the opportunity to develop deeper sector specialization over time.
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which mean meaningful team exposure, including to very seasoned and experienced senior bankers, and the opportunity to make a valued contribution and have an impact.
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation, strategic thinking and client management.
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels.
  • Opportunity to participate in our US and European Secondment Programs.
  • Mentoring Scheme, onboarding sessions and structured developmental feedback.
  • The chance to build your career within a growth environment.

Qualifications:

The Associate position at Lincoln International offers an excellent learning experience and provides a strong foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should bring the following requirements:

  • Approximately 3 years or more of relevant experience in a lead advisory M&A role within an investment bank, top-tier boutique advisory firm or leading accounting firm.
  • Strong transaction execution experience across sell-side and buy-side processes, with healthcare or pharma services exposure preferred.
  • Demonstrable experience preparing client materials, managing execution workstreams, performing financial analysis and supporting transaction process management.
  • Excellent financial modelling, valuation and analytical skills, coupled with strong attention to detail.
  • An exceptional, well-rounded academic background; a professional qualification such as ACA, ACCA, CFA or equivalent is advantageous but not required.
  • AI-forward thinking, with hands-on experience using generative AI tools in a professional setting to improve research, analysis, drafting and workflow efficiency.
  • Clear and engaging communication skills, verbally as well as written, with the ability to relate and connect to clients, management teams and Lincoln colleagues quickly.
  • Strong interpersonal skills, a collaborative style and the ability to manage professional relationships effectively.
  • Excellent written and spoken English skills; additional European languages are advantageous.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and relevant research tools such as Mergermarket, S&P Capital IQ, GainPro and Pitchbook.

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

jobs.title: Capital Advisory Internship (Q1 2027 - Master's Students Only)
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in our offices Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays.

In Germany, our team comprises over 90 bankers covering a very broad range of sectors, including Industrials & Renewables, Consumer, Tech, Business Services, Healthcare, as well as Capital Advisory, located in Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. We are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell sides for private equity clients. In 2025, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 430+ transactions globally.

To support our well-established and growing Capital Advisory Group (CAG) in Frankfurt, which has completed over 170 transactions in the last three years, we are looking for curious and driven students in their Master's or at the end of their Bachelor's degree, who would like to join us as interns for 3-6 months. Internship start will be January 2027. Our internships in the Frankfurt office start on a quarterly basis and offer the chance to gain hands-on experience and valuable exposure to high-level financial advisory work, providing you as intern with the opportunity to develop key skills in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

As part of the team, you will be equipped with a structured pre- and onboarding as well as development opportunities throughout the course of your internship – including for example our induction day, the mentoring program, intern pitch competition and networking events.

Duties & Responsibilities:

You will receive the opportunity to work alongside experienced bankers and be involved in all aspects of the variety across Capital Advisory transactions - thereby develop a solid understanding of Corporate Finance processes. Key responsibilities include the following:

  • Conducting market, industry and company research
  • Supporting deal execution and contributing to the drafting of marketing materials such as teasers, information memorandums, and other relevant documentation,
  • Carrying out financial analyses and assisting with assessments of capital structures, identifying suitable financing parties, participating in the due diligence and closing processes
  • Involvement in pitch presentation preparation
  • Supporting senior bankers with business development and meeting preparation

What else you can look forward to:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding extensive learning opportunities ranging from marketing efforts and origination to live projects and execution 
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, which means working in small deal teams within one or two sector groups, being able to grab different business models and experience a variety of different deal situations
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant team exposure to seasoned bankers and the opportunity to make valuable contributions
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation and strategic thinking
  • Wallstreet Prep/Training the Street, access to our Lincoln Learning Platform and our own Lincoln AI tool as development support
  • Networking opportunities, including senior bankers

Following the successful completion of the internship, select candidates will receive offers to join as full time Analysts.

Qualifications:

  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background, ideally in your final year of studies, with an openness to explore the opportunity of transitioning into a full-time Analyst role following the internship
  • A genuine curiosity for business models and sectors, coupled with a good understanding of corporate strategy and themes, related to company growth and development
  • Previous internships in Corporate Finance related fields that have nurtured your interest in accounting, Finance, M&A, and credit related fields which you know would like to develop further
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn.
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal growth, whether through academic excellence, creative pursuits, or personal projects.
  • Adaptability, dependability, and the ability to manage a dynamic workload
  • Clear communication skills, with the ability to collaborate well with others, and a personally engaging manner
  • An array of international experiences and perspectives
  • Excellent written and spoken German and English skills, additional European Languages are a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint

Important: In order to be considered, please attach a transcript of your university grades as well as any written references from previous internships (if applicable) to your cover letter. 

Please note: All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in-office Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Lincoln International is seeking Analysts to join our rapidly growing Valuations & Opinions Group (VOG). Successful candidates have strong cognitive and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading valuations practice.

The Analyst position within the Valuations & Opinions Group provides an excellent opportunity to become an expert in valuations. The Analyst serves as the analytical and organizational anchor for the Valuations & Opinions Group. Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform. Successful Analysts are assertive, motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Preparing, completing, and ensuring the accuracy of financial models and sophisticated valuation analyses
  • Writing valuation reports and presentations
  • Valuation and analysis of amongst other things, mortgage whole loans, student loans, consumer loans as well as RMBS, CMBS, CLO, ABS, and other structured finance securities
  • Development and maintenance of valuation models for complex financial instruments including private structured finance securities
  • Coordinating recurring valuation projects with team members across multiple geographies
  • Interacting with clients and preparing deliverables
  • Maintaining proprietary valuation databases
  • Tracking the capital markets and analyzing any changes within the markets
  • Preparing marketing materials for meetings with potential clients
  • Supporting the senior members of the Valuations & Opinions Group

Qualifications:

The Analyst position at Lincoln International is extremely challenging, offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. 

  • Six months to two years of structured products experience preferred
  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Mathematics, Engineering, or Economics from top tier institution.
    • Strong academic record of 3.5 GPA or above (both undergraduate and graduate, if applicable)
  • Proficiency at financial modeling with advanced Microsoft Excel skills
  • Strong writing skills and experience with PowerPoint and Microsoft Word
  • Effective organizational skills and the aptitude to manage people and work products
  • Strong communication skills and experience interacting with teammates and clients
  • One year of professional experience in valuations, corporate finance, fixed income or accounting is strongly preferred

Professional and Cultural Characteristics:

  • Authentic individual with high integrity, whose personal and professional values are consistent with Lincoln’s Culture Statement (Excellence, Entrepreneurship, Integration, Collaboration, Integrity)
  • Assertive, articulate, and self-motivated, can manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong collaborator who enjoys working in an entrepreneurial environment
  • Driven and ambitious professional who gains satisfaction from achieving personal and team goals
  • Ability to demonstrate good judgment and handle highly confidential information in a professional manner

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

The salary range for this role is $95,000 to $105,000 on an annual basis.  This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting.  We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future.  This range is only applicable for jobs to be performed in New York City, Chicago, or Dallas.  An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.  

This job is also eligible for bonus pay.

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits to eligible employees.

You can apply for this role through LinkedIn or Lincoln’s Company Page, or through Lincoln’s Internal Jobs Board if you are a current employee.

Note: No amount of pay is considered to be wages or compensation until such amount is earned, vested, and determinable. The amount and availability of any bonus, commission, or any other form of compensation that are allocable to a particular employee remains in the Company's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified at the Company’s sole discretion, consistent with the law.

  • Coordinating recurring valuation projects with team members including providing oversight to analysts

  • Proficiency at financial modeling with advanced Microsoft Excel skills
  • Two to five years of structured products experience
  • Structuring, trading, or whole loan modelling experience desirable
  • Strong writing skills and experience with PowerPoint and Microsoft Word
  • Effective organizational skills and the aptitude to manage people and work products
  • Strong communication skills and experience interacting with teammates and clients

Duties & Responsibilities:

Lincoln International is seeking an Associate to join our rapidly growing Transaction Opinions & Board Advisory team within our Valuations & Opinions Group (VOG). Successful candidates will have strong technical and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading transaction opinions practice in a fast-paced, growth environment. 

The Transaction Opinions Associate role provides an excellent opportunity to gain transactional experience and build on your existing technical finance skills through a mix of engagements for public and private companies as well as leading private equity firms in connection with M&A, restructuring, recapitalization, and spin-off transactions as well as board or special committee advisory situations where we are delivering advisory services, including fairness and solvency opinions or valuations. Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform. Successful Associates are assertive, motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.

Associates are critical deal team members and hold primary responsibility for execution-related workstreams, including information review, construction of complex financial models, drafting and review of board-level presentations, and primary review of analyst workstreams. 

Key Responsibilities include:

  • Preparing, completing, and reviewing financial models and sophisticated valuation and financial analyses in support of fairness and solvency opinions, including discounted cash flow analyses, market-based comparable company and precedent transaction analyses, and three-statement modeling
  • Having primary ownership for the development and review of board-level presentations and reports
  • Coordinating engagement execution and timelines, at the instruction of senior deal team members, and providing oversight to analysts
  • Reviewing information and tracking open items across active engagements, preparing diligence agendas to support diligence processes and inform in-process valuations and analyses
  • Coordinating with clients and, at times, leading diligence efforts or information gathering processes
  • Preparing marketing materials for meetings with potential or existing clients and supporting the senior members of the Transaction Opinions & Board Advisory team

Qualifications:

The Associate position at Lincoln International is extremely challenging, offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career.

  • Excellent analytic foundation with advanced understanding of financial statements and various valuation methodologies (DCF, LBO, comparable company analysis, etc.)
  • Advanced financial modeling and Microsoft Excel skills
  • Strong writing skills and experience with PowerPoint and Microsoft Word
  • Effective organizational skills and the aptitude to manage people and work products
  • Strong communication skills and experience interacting with teammates and clients
  • Three to five years of professional experience in the financial services industry, such as investment banking or valuations is strongly preferred
  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting or Economics from top tier institution; strong academic record (both undergraduate and graduate)
  • Active CPA and/or minimum of CFA Level I is preferred, but not required

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in our offices Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays.

In Belgium, we are a generalist M&A team that covers a broad range of sectors, including Industrials, Consumer, Tech, Business Services and Healthcare. Europe-wide, we are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell-sides for private equity clients. In 2025, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 420 transactions worldwide.

Duties & Responsibilities:

To support our team in Brussels, we are looking for curious and driven individual who would like to join us as interns for 3-5 months. Internship start will be May 2026. Our internship in Brussels offers the chance to gain hands-on experience and valuable exposure to high-level financial advisory work, providing you as intern with the opportunity to develop key skills in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

What else you can look forward to:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding extensive learning opportunities ranging from marketing efforts and origination to live projects and execution
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, which means working in small deal teams within one or two sector groups, being able to grab different business models and experience a variety of different deal situations
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant team exposure to seasoned bankers and the opportunity to make valuable contributions
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation and strategic thinking
  • Wallstreet Prep/Training the Street, access to our Lincoln Learning Platform and our own Lincoln AI tool as development support

Key Responsibilities:

You will receive the opportunity to work alongside experienced bankers and be involved in all aspects of an M&A transaction - thereby develop a solid understanding of M&A processes.

Key responsibilities include the following:

  • Conducting market, industry and company research
  • Supporting deal execution and contributing to the drafting of marketing materials such as teasers, information memorandums, and other relevant documentation
  • Carrying out financial analyses and assisting with valuations, identifying a buyer universe, participating in the due diligence and closing processes
  • Involvement in pitch presentation preparation
  • Supporting senior bankers with business development and meeting preparation

Qualifications:

  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background
  • A genuine curiosity for business models and sectors, coupled with a good understanding of corporate strategy and themes, related to company growth and development
  • Previous internships in Corporate Finance related fields that have nurtured your interest in accounting, Finance, M&A, and valuation which you know would like to develop further
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn.
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal growth, whether through academic excellence, creative pursuits, or personal projects.
  • Adaptability, dependability, and the ability to manage a dynamic workload
  • Clear communication skills, with the ability to collaborate well with others, and a personally engaging manner
  • An array of international experiences and perspectives
  • Excellent written and spoken Dutch and English skills, French is considered a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Lincoln International’s Valuations & Opinions Group, is an industry leader in the valuation of alternative and illiquid investments for investment funds, including business development companies (BDCs), hedge funds, and private equity groups.  The group also advises on valuation matters for corporate transactions, including acquisitions and divestitures, leveraged buyouts, restructurings, recapitalizations, related party transactions, and for general tax, accounting, litigation, and regulatory purposes.

Lincoln International is seeking a Vice President to join the Valuations & Opinions Group with fixed income structured product experience.  Officers work closely with the firm’s Managing Directors and clients in providing valuation and financial advisory services across multiple engagements and offices. Lincoln International’s practice is growing rapidly, and we are looking to add a talented individual to support our client service and quality control activities, expand our capabilities and bolster our marketing efforts. Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform. A successful candidate will be an assertive, motivated self-starter with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment. Prior investment banking and/or transaction experience is preferred but not required.

Key Responsibilities include:

  • Leading project teams providing valuation and financial advisory services to clients, including determining appropriate valuation methodologies and reviewing financial models for amongst other things, mortgage whole loans, student loans, consumer loans as well as RMBS, CMBS, CLO, ABS, and other structured finance securities
  • Providing quality control oversight to engagement teams
  • Managing, training and developing associates and analysts at the firm
  • Proactive calling on potential new clients including alternative investment managers (hedge funds, private equity, private credit, BDCs, etc.), financial institutions, privately held business owners and publicly traded companies
  • Identifying and communicating potential engagements to management and writing business proposals and engagement letters

Qualifications:

  • Minimum of five years’ experience in performing business valuations, valuations of illiquid and complex securities, and/or structuring, trading, or whole loan modelling
  • Understanding of collateral risk characteristics, deal structures, terms, and conditions
  • Experience with third party systems such as Bloomberg and Intex
  • Proficiency in designing financial models for discounted cash flow, market multiple, market transaction, and option pricing analyses
  • Ability to develop valuation approaches for various forms of securities and unique instruments including equity and debt securities, loans, derivative instruments, structured products and intangible assets
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills and ability to critically analyze companies and understand key business issues, including value drivers and investment risks
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Working knowledge of credit markets
  • Experience with successfully managing teams of associates and analysts
  • Understanding of authoritative guidance related to valuations a plus
  • ASC-820 and IFRS 13, valuation industry best practices, and emerging issues
  • Ability to act as a primary point of contact with clients
  • Ability to effectively multi-task, managing multiple client engagements simultaneously
  • Confidently and credibly connect with potential new clients and lead client meetings
  • Excellence in MS Word, Excel and PowerPoint
  • Bachelor’s or Master's degree in Finance, Accounting, Mathematics, Statistics, or Economics or MBA; strong academic record (both undergraduate and graduate)
  • Active CPA, ASA and/or CFA preferred but not required

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

The salary range for this role is $250,000 on an annual basis. This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting.  We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future.  This range is only applicable for jobs to be performed in New York City, Chicago, or Dallas.  An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.  

This job is also eligible for bonus pay.

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits to eligible employees.

You can apply for this role through LinkedIn or Lincoln’s Company Page, or through Lincoln’s Internal Jobs Board if you are a current employee.

This role entails working closely with the firm’s Managing Directors and Directors in building relationships with clients, potential clients, and finance providers. This individual will need to have deep experience of executing financing transactions in the mid-market and will be expected to contribute to Business Development as well as Associates’ and Analysts’ growth at the firm.

In Germany, our team comprises over 90 bankers covering a very broad range of sectors, including Industrials & Renewables, Consumer, Technology, Business Services, Healthcare, as well as Capital Advisory, located in Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. We are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell sides for private equity clients. In 2023, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 300 transactions worldwide.

The European Lincoln International Capital Advisory Group successfully advised on 350+ transactions worldwide since its inception in the range of Euro 50 to 350 million. In 2024 we had another record performance of our global Capital Advisory Group by providing creative and alternative financing solutions to clients out of 5 European offices with more than 25 dedicated professionals in order to secure capital for acquisition financings, investments, refinancings & recapitalisations, staple financings staving off defaults, restructuring businesses and navigating distressed situations and bankruptcies, and developing structured capital solutions.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform.

  • Conceptualising, organising, processing and delivering new business presentations
  • Contributing to transaction process planning and deal structuring conversations
  • Producing and reviewing transaction marketing materials and complex integrated financial models;
  • Managing Analysts and Associates to ensure timelines are met and quality is maintained
  • Acting as key client point of contact for day-to-day queries, with an ability to discuss deal issues internally and with third parties
  • Identifying and contacting finance providers on transactions
  • Preparing term sheets and reviewing transaction due diligence reports and loan agreements
  • Training and developing Associates and Analysts at the firm
  • Contributing to the team and the firm’s marketing efforts as well as initiatives focused on growing the firm’s franchise value.

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Vice President to make a significant impact. For this role, we are looking for individuals with the potential to develop into senior professionals within the organisation over time.

The following factors differentiate the offering at Lincoln International:
  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, working in small deal teams, across multiple countries and embedded in a European team with extensive client exposure as well as cross border contexts
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant senior banker exposure and the opportunity to make a significant impact
  • A steep learning curve in client interaction, project management and strategic thinking
  • Exposure to a vast array of industries
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels
  • Mentoring Scheme, coaching and structured developmental feedback
  • The chance to build your career towards more senior levels within a growth environment

Qualifications:

The Vice President position at Lincoln International offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should bring the following requirements:

  • Extensive track record of executing financing transactions, preferably in the mid-market, with at least 5.5, ideally 6-7 years’ experience in Capital Advisory/Leverage Finance. 
  • Experience and demonstrated ability in seeking and identifying new business opportunities in concert with Managing Directors.
  • Ability to confidently and credibly connect with potential new clients and to lead client meetings.
  • Excellent finance, accounting and financial modeling skills, coupled with pronounced business acumen and the ability to form clear commercial views.
  • Experience in successfully managing teams of Associates and Analysts; and leading project work.
  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background, ideally educated to Masters level in a business related subject. 
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn.
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal and team growth, underpinned through professional and academic excellence as well as other pursuits and personal projects.
  • Adaptability, dependability and the ability to manage a dynamic workload.
  • Clear and engaging communication skills- verbally as well as written. Ability to relate and connect to other stakeholders quickly- clients, management teams and LI colleagues.
  • Collaborative style and a personally engaging manner.
  • International experiences and perspectives. 
  • Excellent written and spoken German and English skills, other European Languages are a plus.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in our offices Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays.

Lincoln International is seeking an experienced Vice President to join our European Healthcare team in London. The successful candidate will combine strong execution expertise with emerging origination capabilities and a passion for building a leading healthcare M&A franchise across Europe. The role offers exposure across healthcare services, pharma services and the broader healthcare value chain, with responsibility for leading transactions and developing client relationships in a dynamic, cross-border environment.

We are looking for AI-forward individuals who are comfortable using AI tools in a professional environment. This includes effective prompting and developing practical agentic workflows to improve research, drafting, analysis and process execution, while applying rigorous quality control, human judgement and appropriate confidentiality standards.

Duties & Responsibilities:

As a Vice President, you will play a central role across transaction execution, client management and business development, acting as the key link between senior bankers and junior team members.

  • Transaction Execution & Leadership
    • Lead the execution of M&A transactions end-to-end, often across multiple concurrent processes.
    • Take responsibility for process management, including timelines, coordination of advisors and delivery of key milestones.
    • Produce and review transaction marketing materials, financial models and valuation analyses.
    • Lead due diligence workstreams, including data room management, Q&A and interaction with clients, counterparties and advisers.
  • Client & Business Development
    • Act as a key day-to-day client contact, supporting relationship development with management teams and investors.
    • Contribute to business development through preparation of pitches, sector insights and participation in client meetings.
  • Team Leadership & AI Enablement
    • Manage, train and develop Associates and Analysts, ensuring high-quality output and timely delivery.
    • Apply AI tools and practical workflows to enhance execution efficiency, while maintaining appropriate oversight and judgement

Why Lincoln:

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Associate to develop as quickly as they are capable. The following factors differentiate the Associate program at Lincoln International:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this.
  • A true mid-cap approach, working in small deal teams with extensive client exposure as well as cross-border contexts.
  • Exposure to a broad range of healthcare subsectors, with the opportunity to develop deeper sector specialization over time.
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which mean meaningful team exposure, including to very seasoned and experienced senior bankers, and the opportunity to make a valued contribution and have an impact.
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation, strategic thinking and client management.
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels.
  • Opportunity to participate in our US and European Secondment Programs.
  • Mentoring Scheme, onboarding sessions and structured developmental feedback.
  • The chance to build your career within a growth environment.

Qualifications:

  • 6–10 years of relevant experience in M&A advisory, with a strong track record of executing transactions; healthcare experience preferred
  • Experience managing transaction processes and leading multiple workstreams concurrently
  • Demonstrated ability to support business development and contribute to client relationship building
  • Strong financial modelling, valuation and analytical skills, with solid business judgement
  • Experience managing and developing Analysts and Associates
  • Clear and effective communication skills, with the ability to engage confidently with clients and colleagues
  • Proactive and self-motivated approach, with the ability to operate in a dynamic environment
  • Familiarity with AI tools and an interest in applying them to improve research, analysis and execution efficiency

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

jobs.description:
