Investment Management strives to deliver exceptional investment portfolios for some of the world’s most influential families and an array of mission-driven institutions. Our goal is to compound returns from diverse sources by harnessing the differentiated capital and strategic strength of the ICONIQ community, cultivated on a foundation of uncommon care.

The Opportunity: Associate, Investment Specialist

This role sits at the intersection of ICONIQ’s Investment Strategy and Client Advisory businesses, playing a critical role in shaping how our Multi-Strategy Investment Management platform is communicated and delivered. The platform spans public and private markets, including private equity, venture capital, co-investments, credit, real estate, real assets, hedge funds, and liquid strategies and is focused on developing differentiated investment solutions while leveraging ICONIQ’s global network to drive exceptional client outcomes.

The Investment Specialist will partner closely with investment teams, portfolio management, and cross-functional stakeholders to support investor relations, investment strategy, and platform communications by translating complex investment strategies, portfolio insights, and performance outcomes into clear, compelling narratives for internal stakeholders, clients, and prospective partners.

In addition, the role will serve as a central hub for content creation and organization, developing scalable resources across key areas such as investment strategy, portfolio construction, cash flow management, and broader family office solutions. This work will help ensure consistency, clarity, and impact in how ICONIQ’s platform is articulated across audiences.

Principal Responsibilities

  • Develop a deep understanding of ICONIQ’s investment platform, including portfolio construction, asset allocation, cash flow dynamics, and strategies across public and private markets
  • Translate complex investment strategies, portfolio positioning, and performance insights into clear, compelling materials tailored for sophisticated investors
  • Partner closely with investment and portfolio management teams to accurately represent investment views, attribution, and market positioning
  • Act as a centralized hub for investment and client content, building and maintaining a comprehensive repository of materials across investment strategies, portfolio management, cash flow planning, and family office needs
  • Create and refine investor-facing materials including pitchbooks, portfolio reviews, white papers, and performance updates
  • Support investor relations efforts by preparing materials for client meetings, responding to investment-related inquiries, and ensuring timely, high-quality communication
  • Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and consistency across materials, including leveraging AI tools and technologies
  • Collaborate with the Client Advisory business to train and develop team members in investment strategies and client-facing capabilities.

Qualifications

  • 4+ years of relevant experience in investor relations, investment management, portfolio management, investment marketing, or a related field
  • Strong understanding of capital markets, investment strategies, portfolio construction, and performance analysis across asset classes
  • Experience developing investor-facing materials such as pitchbooks, portfolio reviews, and performance reports
  • Proven ability to work closely with investment professionals and operate effectively in a highly analytical environment
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and results-driven with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to collaborate effectively across teams and time zones
  • Familiarity with AI tools and technologies is a plus; interest in leveraging AI to enhance productivity, streamline workflows, and scale content development
  • Demonstrated ability to work cooperatively and collaboratively across all levels of the organization to exceed client expectations

Location: Applicants are expected to work onsite in our New York City office in accordance with our hybrid working policy.

Compensation: ICONIQ is required by law to include a reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role, together with other compensation being offered such as a discretionary bonus, health insurance, and other perks. At ICONIQ, we apply a total reward philosophy where all aspects of compensation and benefits are taken into consideration in determining compensation terms. jobs.title: Senior Analyst- Associate, Financial Advisory (New York and San Francisco)
jobs.description:
Senior Analyst or Associate, Financial Advisory (Talent Pipeline)

As a Senior Analyst or Associate within our Financial Advisory (FA) team, you will operate at the intersection of legacy and innovation, serving as a trusted partner to some of the world’s most influential individuals and families. You will help clients navigate the complexities of their financial lives with precision, empathy, and uncommon care, ensuring that every element of their balance sheet, investment strategy, and long-term plan is managed with clarity and foresight.

You will be joining a collaborative advisory team that values learning, partnership and shared ownership. We work closely across levels and alongside specialists so that every client benefits from the full strength of the ICONIQ platform.

This is a role for professionals who are energized by complexity, motivated by service, and driven to make a lasting impact. If you take pride in anticipating needs, delivering exceptional outcomes, and making complexity feel effortless, this is your opportunity to join a team built on trust, care, and excellence.


Given our ongoing talent needs, we are consistently looking for high quality individuals to join our team. However, please note that this opening is intended to pipeline candidates for future opportunities, and we will reach out when the timing is right.

Core Responsibilities for All Candidates:

You will support clients across a broad set of investment, planning, and relationship activities, including:

  • Preparing and analyzing balance sheets, asset allocations, investment performance reports, and other financial materials.
  • Assisting with investment operations, including trading coordination, cash flow monitoring, and private investment updates.
  • Conducting financial and estate planning analyses such as liquidity modeling, lending evaluations, and gifting illustrations.
  • Partnering with accountants, attorneys, and external advisors to gather information and support planning conversations.
  • Preparing client meeting materials, managing follow ups, and maintaining accurate data in Salesforce, Addepar, and other systems.
  • Collaborating closely with Investment Strategy Group, Legal, Compliance, and Operations to ensure seamless client service.

If You Are a Senior Analyst:

Senior Analysts take on increasing ownership, judgment, and communication responsibility. In addition to the core responsibilities, you will:

  • Own key deliverables such as preparing asset allocation reviews, rebalancing recommendations, and planning analyses.
  • Support advanced planning work, including Monte Carlo simulations, executive compensation summaries, and scenario-based analyses.
  • Build knowledge of trusts, estate strategies, and gifting structures.
  • Participate in parts of client meetings and proactively identify opportunities such as refinancing, liquidity needs, or strategic planning considerations.
  • Mentor Analysts on processes, systems, and best practices as you grow into a leadership mindset.

Sr. Analyst Qualifications:
A bachelor’s degree and 3+ years of relevant experience in financial services or wealth management.

If You Are an Associate:

Associates deepen their role as strategic advisors and trusted client partners. You will:

  • Lead client relationships in partnership with senior team members, helping clients interpret investment results, plan across generations, and make informed decisions.
  • Drive portfolio discussions, rebalancing recommendations, and investment rationale with a strong understanding of asset classes and the ICONIQ investment philosophy.
  • Guide trust and estate planning conversations and coordinate with attorneys on structures, gifting strategies, and long-term planning.
  • Manage complex client processes such as 10b5 1 plans, lending evaluations, private investment coordination, and other high impact deliverables.
  • Coach junior teammates, strengthen team workflows, and help elevate the quality and clarity of client outputs.

Associate Qualifications:
A bachelor’s degree and 5+ years of experience working with high net worth or ultra-high net worth clients.

Who You Are:

Across both levels, successful candidates demonstrate:

  • Strong analytical and communication skills and comfort explaining complex topics simply.
  • Strong attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy.
  • Intellectual curiosity, proactive thinking, and a passion for client service.
  • High integrity in handling confidential and sensitive information.
  • A collaborative mindset and enthusiasm for working as part of a team.

Location: Applicants are expected to work onsite in our New York or San Francisco office in accordance with our hybrid working policy.

Compensation Range:  ICONIQ is required by law to include a reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role. There are several factors taken into consideration in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. At ICONIQ, we apply a total reward philosophy where all aspects of compensation and benefits are taken into consideration in determining compensation terms. A reasonable estimate of the current salary range is $110,000 - $120,000 for Senior Analysts and $130,000 - $150,000 for Associates together with a discretionary bonus and the benefits listed above.

jobs.description:
ICONIQ Venture & Growth is seeking a Senior Analyst to join its Portfolio Operations team, a highly strategic function focused on driving commercial and strategic value across our portfolio and broader ecosystem. This role partners closely with founders, executives, and enterprise leaders to unlock growth opportunities, generate actionable insights, and strengthen our network advantage.

This role sits within ICONIQ Venture & Growth which partners with founders defining the future of their industries to transform the world.

What You’ll Do:

  • Expand and map ICONIQ’s network of senior enterprise leaders to drive long-term ecosystem strength
  • Build relationships with enterprise leaders through events, strategic discussions, and ongoing market engagement
  • Support go-to-market efforts for portfolio companies by identifying target customers and facilitating strategic introductions
  • Partner with founders and executives to refine customer profiles, target sectors, and growth priorities
  • Develop market insights by analyzing enterprise buying trends and emerging technology adoption
  • Design and execute curated executive engagements including roundtables, briefings, and industry events
  • Maintain accurate data and reporting to track ecosystem growth and portfolio impact
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to deliver high-quality outcomes and strengthen internal knowledge sharing

  • Strong academic track record and analytical capabilities

  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage senior stakeholders
  • Proficiency with tools such as Excel, PowerPoint, Google Workspace, or Salesforce

jobs.title: 2027 Summer Analyst
jobs.description:
The Firm:

Lead Edge Capital is a $9 billion growth equity ﬁrm backing software, internet, and tech-enabled businesses around the world. Since 2011, we’ve partnered with over 100 companies, including Alibaba, Asana, Toast, Duo Security, Wise, and Benchling, and have seen more than 60 successful exits. We look for companies where our network can make a diﬀerence, and where speed, access, and execution matter. We’re powered by a global network of 700+ operators, executives, and entrepreneurs who do more than commit capital. They help our companies hire, grow, and win. Lead Edge has oﬃces in New York, London, and Santa Barbara.

Overview:

The Lead Edge Analyst Program is a summer internship designed to provide meaningful exposure to the buy-side investment landscape during junior summer. Summer Analysts play an integral role in generating deal flow and evaluating potential investments, working closely with Senior Investment Partners and Associates. Throughout the internship, Analysts engage in numerous one-on-one conversations with entrepreneurs to help source new investment opportunities for the firm. When an Analyst identifies a promising opportunity, they become deeply involved in the diligence and evaluation process, contributing meaningfully to investment discussions and decision-making.

Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Perform market research and collaborate with LEC’s network to develop insights into various markets
  • Build and maintain relationships with founders and management teams through proactive sourcing efforts
  • Screen deals and support team members in conducting quantitative analyses, including financial modeling and retention analysis
  • Maintain a pipeline of investment opportunities to present to team members and senior leadership
  • Creating materials and analysis for evaluation by LEC’s investment committee
  • Performing ﬁnancial modeling and valuation analysis
  • Monitor and support portfolio companies, providing operational insight

Requirements:

  • Strong interest in growth equity, and technology investing
  • Excellent analytical and critical-thinking skills; detail-oriented, proactive, and highly self-motivated
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships with entrepreneurs, founders, and teammates
  • Collaborative mindset and comfort working independently or on lean teams
  • Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
  • Proficient with the Microsoft Office suite
  • 3.5 GPA or higher
  • U.S. work authorization required

Compensation:

  • The budgeted base salary range is $100,000 pro-rated over the length of the internship.

The Originations group at Lead Edge focuses on deal generation, with an emphasis on intermediary relationships, and supporting the broader firm in identifying new opportunities.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Primary responsibilities of an Originations Analyst will be assisting with the operations of the Originations efforts such as internal reporting, CRM management, analysis projects on coverage, and process shepherding, as well as supporting outward-facing sourcing efforts such as relationship building and entertainment. With a small team today, the Originations team represents a growth opportunity within Lead Edge.

  • Develop a strong understanding of internal systems and workflows to ensure proper sourcing documentation
  • Own end-to-end data management, including data entry, maintenance, and reporting.
  • Conduct data-driven analyses on sourcing efforts to help guide coverage decisions
  • Support with “Air Traffic Control” developing the right institutional knowledge of LEC to make the best connections to external parties
  • Assist in preliminary processing and review of investment opportunities

  • Participate in entertainment such as in-person meetings, events, dinners, etc.

  • The budgeted base salary range is $100,000-$125,000. Total compensation will include base salary and performance-based bonus. jobs.title: Senior Credit Underwriting Analyst
jobs.description:
The word ‘credit’ is derived from the latin verb meaning, “one believes”. Underwriting is not merely a science of risk scoring and algorithms, but also an art of unearthing hidden variables, and of subtle pattern recognition; bridging the gap between that of real and perceived risk. The established lending frameworks shun the intrepid entrepreneur, deeming them too risky, and lacking certain credibility. Fortunate for them, Mercury provides banking* for these ambitious startups, and they are in need of believers. 

As a Senior Credit Underwriter, you will be joining a team of lending experts with experience spanning unsecured cards to secured commercial credit. You will help shape our Working Capital loan product designed for eCommerce, software and managed services companies with a vision to extend to all small businesses. You will work directly with prospective borrowers to assess creditworthiness and structure creative lending solutions to support their growth. You will also partner with sales and product teams to innovate the go to market process, underwriting, and portfolio management needs of the rapidly scaling loan program. 

*Mercury is a fintech company, not an FDIC-insured bank. Banking services provided through Choice Financial Group and Column N.A., Members FDIC.

_____________________________________________________________________________________

As part of the journey, we would expect you to: 

  • Underwrite loan application independently: Analyze quantitative and qualitative data across ecommerce, SaaS, and SMB borrowers. Structure, price, and propose loan terms that balance repayment capacity and Mercury’s risk posture.
  • Own the customer-facing deal motion: Lead diligence calls with prospective borrowers, co-pilot active negotiations with AEs, provide real-time eligibility and structuring guidance, and support the sales team on complex or competitive applications from first touch through close.
  • Monitor portfolio health: Support ongoing covenant monitoring, delinquency tracking, and portfolio review processes. Contribute to monthly portfolio reviews with early warning signal identification.
  • Contribute to credit standards: Support drafting and refinement of underwriting guidelines, risk rating frameworks, and policy documentation.
  • Work cross-functionally: Partner with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Data Science to improve the underwriting process, customer experience, and credit infrastructure.

What we are looking for:

  • 5+ years of credit or underwriting experience in Fintech, SMB, or commercial lending, with meaningful customer-facing experience
  • Strong credit fundamentals: financial statement analysis, risk rating, cash flow assessment, and identification of structural mitigants across diverse business models.
  • Direct deal experience: has been in the room with customers during negotiations, underwriting calls, and structuring conversations — not just behind the analysis.
  • Builder mindset: comfortable operating in a high-growth environment where process, tooling, and infrastructure are still being defined. 
  • Cross-functional fluency: experience working alongside Sales, Legal, Product, and Engineering. Able to translate credit requirements into language that lands across teams.
  • Exposure to regulated lending or institutional credit frameworks is a plus. Familiarity with credit file standards, lending policy documentation, and examination-ready governance practices is helpful as Mercury approaches bank charter, not a hard requirement.

The total rewards package at Mercury includes base salary, equity (stock options), and benefits.

Our salary and equity ranges are highly competitive within the SaaS and fintech industry and are updated regularly using the most reliable compensation survey data for our industry. New hire offers are made based on a candidate’s experience, expertise, geographic location, and internal pay equity relative to peers.

Our target new hire base salary ranges for this role are the following:

  • US employees located in New York City, Los Angeles, Seattle, or the San Francisco Bay Area: $142,900 - $160,700
  • US employees located outside of New York City, Los Angeles, Seattle or the San Francisco Bay Area: $128,600 - $144,600
  • Canadian employees (any location): CAD $135,000 - $151,900

Mercury values diversity & belonging and is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. All individuals seeking employment at Mercury are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristic. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process for applicants with disabilities or special needs. If you need assistance, or an accommodation, please let your recruiter know once you are contacted about a role.

We use Covey as part of our hiring and / or promotional process for jobs in NYC and certain features may qualify it as an AEDT. As part of the evaluation process we provide Covey with job requirements and candidate submitted applications. We began using Covey Scout for Inbound on January 22, 2024.

[Please see the independent bias audit report covering our use of Covey for more information.]

jobs.title: Licensed Investment Services Consultant - PNC Wealth Management
jobs.description:
Position Overview

At PNC, our people are our greatest differentiator and competitive advantage in the markets we serve. We are all united in delivering the best experience for our customers. We work together each day to foster an inclusive workplace culture where all of our employees feel respected, valued and have an opportunity to contribute to the company’s success. This position may be eligible for remote work in select geographic locations, subject to approval by PNC. If approved, work must be conducted from a quiet, secure, and confidential home-based workspace. Occasional in-office participation may be required based on business needs.

The Investment Services Consultant (ISC) role within PNC Wealth Management acts as a phone-based sales and service financial advisor within our fast-paced Investment Center. You will be aligned with branch partners from your assigned offices who refer clients seeking advice and guidance. You will engage with clients from the PNC Investment Center book of business to understand their goals and objectives, and recommend investment solutions tailored to their individual situation and needs.This position may be eligible for remote work in select geographic locations, subject to approval by PNC. If approved, work must be conducted from a quiet, secure, and confidential home-based workspace. Occasional in-office participation may be required based on business needs.PNC will not provide sponsorship for employment visas or participate in STEM OPT for this position.

Job Description

  • Actively and regularly coaches and educates internal eco partners to drive investment visibility, identify investment opportunities, and lead team on actions to achieve investment results. Has regular and effective communication with internal and external partners to build strong relationships that drive referral activity. Make referrals to internal business partners as appropriate.
  • Promotes PNC's products and services to existing and prospective clients held either collectively in a book of business or through referred opportunities, demonstrating how PNC can help them achieve their financial goals. Identifies opportunities through meaningful conversations with customers. Develop and implement investment and insurance strategies and provide sound guidance for clients by recommending the best products to support such strategies. Applies product and procedural knowledge to solve client problems appropriately and efficiently. Owns and manages the end-to-end client experience, efficiently handling service transactions and identifying and executing sales opportunities. Creates customer loyalty and grows customer share of wallet through a differentiated customer experience. Meets service level agreements on lead and referral follow up as it relates to service and sales opportunities.
  • Adheres to all applicable laws, regulations, policies and guidelines while maintaining accurate, complete and timely documentation for transactional activity completed on client accounts.

PNC Employees take pride in our reputation and to continue building upon that we expect our employees to be:

  • Customer Focused - Knowledgeable of the values and practices that align customer needs and satisfaction as primary considerations in all business decisions and able to leverage that information in creating customized customer solutions.
  • Managing Risk - Assessing and effectively managing all of the risks associated with their business objectives and activities to ensure they adhere to and support PNC's Enterprise Risk Management Framework.

Qualifications

Successful candidates must demonstrate appropriate knowledge, skills, and abilities for a role. Listed below are skills, competencies, work experience, education, and required certifications/licensures needed to be successful in this position.

Preferred Skills

Client Prospecting, Client Relationship Building, Credit Risk Analysis, Customer Engagement, Financial Analysis, Financial Modeling, Investment Banking, Public Speaking, Relationship Building, Relationship Management

Competencies

Accuracy and Attention to Detail, Coaching Others, Customer Experience Management., Customer Retention, Effective Communications, Knowledge Of Customers, Matrix Management, Problem Solving, Products and Services, Prospecting, Relationship Management, Value Selling

The Business Development Associate role at Star Mountain Capital is responsible for supporting the external business development team by driving strategic relationship management initiatives for institutional and wealth clients.   • Proficient knowledge of alternative investments.
  • Excited to work hard in an entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment, building a unique organization.
  • Ability to handle multiple work streams and support senior professionals in complex sales cycles.

Star Mountain is a rapidly growing, specialized private investment firm focused on the large and less competitive lower middle-market (“LMM”), comprised of businesses that generally have between $5 million and $50 million in annual EBITDA. Position Description:

Portfolio Company Oversight

  • Assist senior Underwriting and Portfolio Management professionals in managing portfolio company relationships and preparing for board meetings, including direct support on operational reviews, performance improvement initiatives, and management accountability tracking
  • Review and analyze monthly financials, quarterly covenant compliance certifications, annual audited financial statements from portfolio companies, with a focus on identifying emerging risks, liquidity constraints, and operational inefficiencies
  • Analyze company-level operating metrics and financials data to disaggregate drivers of under/outperformance relative to budget and prior year, developing actionable insights to support margin improvement, cost optimization, and revenue enhancement initiatives
  • Support value creation initiatives (i.e., Getting Middle Market Ready, working capital management, and reporting, ), including operational benchmarking, KPI enhancement, and execution tracking across portfolio companies
  • Provide analytical and operational support during periods of underperformance or disruption, including liquidity stress, covenant pressure, management transitions, or macro-driven volatility (light crisis management)
  • Assist in evaluating and supporting restructuring, reorganization, and turnaround initiatives, including capital structure optimization, cost rationalization efforts, and strategic realignment analyses.

Internal Reporting

  • Maintain historical portfolio company financial, capital structure and qualitative attributes in firm data management platforms (S&P Global iLEVEL and WSO), ensuring data accuracy to support operational decision-making and performance diagnostics
  • Support valuations processes on portfolio company securities on a quarterly basis, including scenario and downside analyses reflecting operational and restructuring considerations
  • Prepare quarterly and semi-annual credit and equity reviews on individual portfolio companies, highlighting operational trends, execution risks, and value creation progress
  • Craft thoughtful responses to investor requests related to portfolio company security details and performance, including explanations of operational initiatives, performance remediation plans, and restructuring outcomes where applicable

Portfolio Financing and Amendment Support

  • Support drafting and presentation of internal amendment and financing memoranda for relevant approvals from Chief Credit Officer and Investment Committee.
  • Prepare detailed scenario-based spreadsheet models to project portfolio company operating and financial performance, including downside, stress-case, and restructuring scenarios, as well as credit statistics and projected returns
  • Assist investment analysts in completing pre-investment and post-close workflows, procedures and documentation, with added involvement in post-close performance monitoring, amendment execution, and reorganization processes
  • Support cross-functional coordination with legal, restructuring advisors, consultants, and portfolio company management teams during amendments, workouts, and operational improvement initiatives

Required Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of investment-related (private equity, private credit), corporate banking, or other finance-related experience (investment banking, accounting, audit, consulting), with exposure to portfolio monitoring, operational analysis, restructurings, or performance improvement initiatives preferred
  • Microsoft Excel power user, ability to build 3 statement LBO models, including experience with scenario modeling, sensitivity analysis, and operational KPIs
  • Excellent organizational skills with a proactive and detail-oriented approach to managing multiple tasks and priorities
  • Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills
  • Team player with a positive attitude and great people skills

Timing: Immediate

Location: Tampa, Florida

jobs.title: 2025-2026 Private Equity Intern
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The team you’ll join

StepStone’s Private Equity (PE) team diligences and invests in opportunities across the private equity landscape. The team will invest directly in companies alongside other sponsors, complete LP and GP - led secondary transactions, and evaluate primary investments on behalf of StepStone’s advisory and discretionary clients. Through analysis of leveraged buyout deals and funds, StepStone’s PE team works across a wide array of industries and capital structures.

About the role

StepStone is currently seeking penultimate and final year undergraduate students or students enrolled in a master’s degree with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an intern position on StepStone’s Private Equity Team. What you’ll do

Interns will work in a team environment to target and perform due diligence, including financial modelling and preparation of investment memorandums, on opportunities across the Private Equity team’s three investment verticals: private equity fund investments, co-investment transactions and secondaries transactions.

Key responsibilities

  • Analyze and conduct diligence on co-investment opportunities and secondary transactions
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Conduct due diligence on potential fund investments (team, strategy, performance), and synthesize findings in an investment memorandum to be presented at the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Speak with senior executives to gather insights regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

What we’re looking for

  • Pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree, expected to graduate in 2026 or later
  • Completed at least one relevant 3- or 6-month internship, and able to work on-site at StepStone’s Beijing office at least four days per week 
  • High proficiency in MS Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills

jobs.title: Analyst / Associate, Infrastructure Private Equity / Responsible Investment
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:

The Analyst / Associate at StepStone Infrastructure & Real Assets will be responsible for providing support across transactional and Responsible Investment (RI/ESG) activity to SIRA’s local and international team in advising clients on investments in infrastructure and associated real asset sectors globally. The role will require analytical and modelling skills, but the candidate is also expected to have a strong understanding of sustainability concepts and issues.

Essential Job Functions:
  • Strong financial modelling and analytical skills – ability to create and/or modify sophisticated financial models, analyze financial statements and financial forecasts, prepare valuation bridges, run discounted cash flow analysis, comparable public companies, and create sensitivity/scenario analysis;
  • Strong memo / presentation writing and preparation skills;
  • Strong client interaction skills – ability to interact with clients and prepare advisory materials as required;
  • Support investment teams on Responsible Investment, including engaging on ESG risks and opportunities during due diligence and developing and delivering training on key issues to colleagues;
  • Develop RI-related materials, including customized presentations for investor meetings, RFP responses, ESG reporting, and legal documentation
  • Ability to undertake research regarding, and diligence of, potential investment opportunities and sectors, including primary funds, secondaries, co-investments and direct investments;
  • Ability to construct an argument around investment decisions and effectively communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend your position in a team environment, including in Investment Committee meetings;
  • Monitor global ESG trends and emerging best practices (e.g., industry and regulatory updates etc.), providing insights to leadership and identifying and executing on enhancements to the firm’s ESG practices

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • 2–5 years of experience in IB, project finance advisory, transaction services, or equivalent with some relevant experience in Responsible Investment (ESG)
  • Relevant buyside and Responsible Investment experience at a FoF or GP would also be considered directly transferable
  • Transaction experience — M&A, financings, or diligence processes with clear deal exposure
  • Ability to produce high-quality (at or near native standard) written content in business English
  • Second European language is preferred (French, German, Danish)
  • Self-starter, eager to learn and desire to grow within the organization
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced global team environment with an ability to manage changing and competing priorities

Other Desired Skills/Attributes:

  • Flexibility – StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Evidence of ESG certification (e.g. CFA Sustainable Investing, IFRS / SASB FSA credential) is a plus, but not required

jobs.description:

Position Overview:         

StepStone Private Wealth Solutions is seeking an Analyst/Associate with excellent quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills to support the product management efforts of financial platforms globally as they consider the suite of funds under management. The team oversees and manages data across five portfolio strategies and controls the information being shared externally.

The Analyst/Associate’s primary role is to draft high-quality, consistent responses, fund materials, and other analyses in response to requests from current and prospective platforms. The Analyst/Associate will work closely with the existing members of the product management team as they learn the process and expectations with the opportunity to begin collaborating with and have exposure to all levels across the firm. The candidate is expected to show a willingness to learn the underlying products and the comparable product landscape to support these efforts appropriately. The person should also have a basic understanding and interest in the private markets investment space (specifically, private equity, infrastructure, real estate, and private debt), strong interpersonal skills and project management skills. The candidate should be highly analytical and naturally curious to understand the underlying data. Strong written communication, organization, attention to detail and relationship building skills will be necessary to succeed in this role.         

Essential Job Functions: 

  • Responsible for completing written responses to inquiries from prospective platforms and existing clients, including writing and editing content, working with cross-functional teams to obtain the necessary information, and ensuring that the final product is accurate and delivered on time.
  • Critically assess the accuracy and relevance of content provided by cross-functional team members in response to DDQs/RFPs.
  • Contribute to the refinement and implementation of internal AI initiatives designed to pre-populate and streamline questionnaire responses
  • Manage and maintain the private wealth central database of responses to ensure content is kept up to date.
  • Respond to ad hoc requests initiated by platforms seeking deeper analysis of performance metrics, portfolio construction, or underlying investments.
  • Collaborate with portfolio managers, BD leadership, investment, legal, compliance, fund accounting, and portfolio analytics teams to synthesize insights across performance drivers, capital deployment dynamics, fundraising trends, portfolio evolution, and competitive positioning.
  • Maintain a high standard of data integrity and presentation quality across all portfolio-related deliverables.
  • Contribute to the development of portfolio narratives and reusable content for quarterly updates and platform communications.
  • Assist with various projects to streamline internal processes.
  • Strong ability to focus on the task at hand with an attention of the final deadline associated with the project or deliverable.

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:         

  • Undergraduate degree / higher education qualification in finance, business, data science, or liberal arts.
  • Experience with related financial services (private equity, investment banking, asset management, investor relations and/or consulting) is a plus.
  • Highly motivated self-starter, with demonstrated history of taking initiative, being adaptive in a fast-changing environment, and working independently.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:          

  • Candidate should be an energetic, self-motivated, and team-oriented individual who thrives in high-performance environments and maintains composure and precision under tight deadlines.
  • Demonstrated intellectual curiosity and commitment to developing deep private markets expertise.
  • Demonstrated interest in financial technology, automation, and AI-enabled workflow optimization.
  • Excellent report writing and communication skills.
  •  High level of confidentiality and understanding regulatory boundaries with sharing information.
  • Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Ability to be flexible with changing priorities and company growth.
  • Ability to take ownership and accountability.

jobs.description:

Position Overview:

StepStone is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an Analyst position on StepStone’s Real Estate Advisory Team. The Analyst’s primary role is to engage in sector-focused research on various areas of the real estate private equity industry such as opportunistic, value-add, core and credit. The Analyst will work in a team environment with other Research team members to target and perform due diligence on selected real estate private equity fund managers; analyze PPMs, financial statements and investment returns; and conduct reference calls and personal interviews. In addition, the Analyst will work with the co-investment and secondary teams to source, diligence and execute transactions. The Analyst will have the opportunity to present recommendations, with the investment team, to the Real Estate Investment Committee.

StepStone prides itself on the Firm’s collegial atmosphere, encouraging team-building initiatives and a collaborative approach to accomplishing projects. The Analyst will have exposure to all levels of the Firm, from Partners to fellow Analysts, daily. The Analyst will often have opportunities to interact with many of the top fund managers in the real estate private equity industry.

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree and backgrounds in real estate, private equity, investment banking, consulting or other relevant industries. The Analyst will adhere to the highest ethical standards, as well as demonstrate maturity, reliability and attentiveness to detail.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Interpret financial analyses prepared by target fund managers, including investment track records, financial statement analysis and fund models
  • Support senior Advisory staff on sourcing and managing the inflow of investment opportunities
  • Assess marketing and supporting materials of prospective fund managers to identify key value drivers and important areas for further investigation
  • Conduct phone interviews with senior executives to gather insights and information regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity
  • Efficiently communicate a position verbally and in written materials, including defending a position in a team environment such as Investment Committee meetings
  • Use creativity to source and analyze data and to test hypotheses regarding attractiveness of specific sectors and investment opportunities
  • Prepare for and participate in meetings with managers and conduct on-going due diligence
  • Perform financial modeling and sensitivity analyses related to co-investment and secondary opportunities
  • Draft investment memorandums and presentations related to fund, co-investment and secondary diligences

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • BA/BS in in Real Estate, Private Equity, Finance, Economics, Accounting, Law or equivalent relevant experience
  • Demonstrated track record of academic and professional success
  • Prior internships or work experience in private equity real estate, investment banking, consulting or other relevant experience
  • Availability to travel, primarily domestically

Other Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:

  • Flexibility to work within StepStone’s dynamic and entrepreneurial organization
  • Developing investment judgment and general understanding of risk/reward trade-offs
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • High proficiency in Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Applied experience in valuation and investment analysis
  • Enrollment in CFA program a plus
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude

jobs.title: Fund Controller - Collateralized Fund Obligation & Structured Products
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview

The Fund Controller, Fund Accounting – CFO & Structured Products will be responsible for accounting, financial reporting, and assist with operational execution for Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) products, other structured credit vehicles, and Private Equity vehicles.  The Fund Controller acts as the internal subject‑matter expert on CFO structures, bridging Fund Accounting, Treasury, Investment Operations, Capital Markets, Legal, Tax, external auditors, trustees, collateral administrators, and rating agencies. The role is designed to reduce execution risk, internalize institutional knowledge, and establish repeatable operating and accounting standards for a growing structured products platform. 

In this position, the Fund Controller will manage a team of accountants and various fund administrators. The Fund Controller will also work closely with the Fund Accounting Treasury and Operations teams along with the Investment Operations team.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Own the end‑to‑end fund accounting and financial reporting framework for CFO vehicles, including master/feeder structures, note issuances, equity tranches, and related SPVs.
  • Serve as an internal expert matter on indenture interpretation and accounting implications, partnering with Legal and auditors on complex or judgmental matters.
  • Review and challenge work prepared by collateral administrators, trustees, and third‑party fund administrators, ensuring outputs align with governing documents and accounting standards.
  • Act as the designated CFO product lead for launches and closings, coordinating high‑volume cash activity and multi‑party workflows
  • Oversee funding mechanics, drawdowns, note interest calculations, waterfall allocations, and reserve accounts.
  • Additionally, manage and oversee a portfolio of Private Equity funds, which includes but is not limited to:
  • Review work prepared by the fund administrator
    • Reviewing accounting entries from the Fund Administrator’s accounting and reporting systems  
    • Reviewing and reconciling cash and investment accounts with General Partner reports  
    • Reviewing of investment partnership reports
    • Reviewing and reconciliation of capital account activity
    • Review quarterly unaudited and annual audited financial statements in accordance with GAAP
    • Coordinate preparation of annual audits with outside CPA firm
    • Review partner allocations and capital account statements
    • Manage process for delivering quarterly and annual investor reports and other ad-hoc communications
  • Understand partnership agreements and interpret reporting requirements and partner economics
  • Review management fees, distribution waterfalls and carried interest for Private Equity vehicles
  • Corresponding with General Partners regarding accounting/reporting questions, or other issues, as necessary    
  • Corresponding with underlying investors regarding questions, requests, etc.
  • Respond to investor requests in a timely manner
  • Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate
  • Perform other duties as required, including but not limited to:
    • Review of K-1s and support for tax reporting and analysis of tax matters
    • Support for the Compliance team, including preparation of materials for ADV, Form PF, SLT, etc.
    • Support for other departments across StepStone

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • Experience with accounting for structured finance, private equity partnership, and fund of funds
  • Direct experience with CFOs, CLOs, NAV‑based facilities, or other structured credit products
  • Deep understanding of fund structures, note issuances, waterfalls, and cash flow mechanics
  • A Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance with 12+ years of professional industry experience
  • Ability to multi-task and manage priorities against fast-paced key deadlines
  • Ideal candidates will have experience with SEC registered investment advisors
  • Must be able to work independently but contribute in a team-oriented environment
  • Previous experience with Investment Company Accounting and ASC 820 (FAS 157) and FIN 48
  • Strong Microsoft Excel experience
  • Experience training and managing junior professionals and reviewing the work of others

Other Attributes: 

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to maintain strict confidentiality of client, investment and company information
  • Ability to problem solve and leverage technology
  • Acute attention to accuracy and detail
  • Professional demeanor and an ability to work well in a team environment
  • Highly proficient in using Excel, Word, and other Microsoft office programs
  • Willingness and ability to learn and implement new programs and processes

Salary Range: $160,000 - $180,000

The Private Equity Analyst will engage broadly in various areas of the private equity industry, including manager and fund diligence for StepStone’s advisory practice, as well as diligence responsibilities for StepStone’s asset management business lines (i.e., co-investment and secondaries practices). 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

  • Work in a team environment with other investment team members to source, screen, and conduct due diligence on select private equity fund managers, co-investments, and secondaries
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Analyze PPMs, financial statements, and investment returns
  • Conduct reference calls as a part of manager and asset due diligence
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Prepare due diligence materials and Investment Committee memos
  • Present recommendations to the investment team and the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Monitor the performance of the Firm’s existing investments

EDUCATION AND/OR WORK EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

  • Must be able to work legally in the U.S. without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • 1 year of investment banking, consulting, and/or private equity experience   
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES: 

  • Excellent quantitative skills, including financial modeling, analytical and valuation skills for purpose of evaluating investment opportunities
  • Strong sense of risk/reward trade-offs, and an ability to display sound investment judgement
  • Self-starter, with strong ability to think creatively and conduct research to find solutions
  • Ability to communicate and present ideas effectively, both verbally and in writing
  • Capable of multi-tasking with strong attention to detail
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude, with desire to work on various projects
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Fluent (written and spoken) English

Salary Range: $105,000 - $112,500

The salary range is an estimate of pay for this position.  Actual pay may vary depending on job-related factors that can include location, education, skill, and experience.  The salary range does not include any benefits or other forms of possible compensation that may be available to employees.

jobs.title: Private Equity Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

About StepStone:

StepStone (Nasdaq: STEP) is a global private markets specialist overseeing over US$723 billion of private capital allocations, including approximately US$199 billion of assets under management.

StepStone Private Equity provides its investors and clients with customized exposure to private equity through its global market relationships, deep research capabilities, granular due diligence, and structuring capabilities. StepStone builds this exposure through its commingled funds, customized funds, separately managed accounts, and investment advisory assignments.

Position Overview:

StepStone is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an Analyst position on StepStone’s Private Equity Investment Team in the Firm’s Beijing or Singapore office. The Analyst’s primary role is to engage in sector-focused research on various areas of the private equity industry such as venture capital, growth, buyout and distressed strategies in Asia. In addition, the Analyst will work in a team environment with other investment team members to target and perform due diligence on selected private companies and private equity funds; analyze PPMs, financial statements and investment returns; and conduct reference calls and personal interviews. The Analyst will have the opportunity to present recommendations, with the investment team, to the Firm’s Investment Committee.

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree and 1+ years of private equity, investment banking, consulting, or other relevant experience. The Analyst will adhere to the highest ethical standards, as well as demonstrate maturity, reliability, and attentiveness to detail.

Essential Job Functions:

· Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

· Interpret financial analyses prepared by target fund managers, including investment track records and financial statement analysis; prepare valuation analyses on private companies using comparable public companies and precedent transactions

· Support senior research staff on sourcing and managing the inflow of investment opportunities

· Assess marketing and supporting materials of prospective fund managers to identify key value drivers and important areas for further investigation

· Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity

· Construct an argument around an opinion and efficiently communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend position in a team environment, including Investment Committee meetings

· Use creativity to source and analyze data and to test hypotheses regarding attractiveness of specific sectors and investment opportunities

· Analyze and monitor the performance of the Firm's existing fund managers and underlying portfolio companies; prepare for and participate in meetings with assigned managers, and conduct on-going due diligence

Other Requirements:

· BA/BS from a leading university; top quartile academic background; demonstrated track record of academic and professional success

· Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications

· Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills

· Leadership potential

· High proficiency in Word, Power Point and Excel

· Flexibility—StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop

· Travel around 5 days per month to regions throughout Asia (Greater China, Korea, Japan, India, Australia, SE Asia, etc.

jobs.description:

Position Overview

StepStone is looking for a customer-centric, detail-oriented individual who will work with a team that manages fund operations and client relations for the StepStone Australia Funds. The person should have a solid understanding of investment operations and strong communication skills.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Manage daily communication with the fund’s Australia transfer agent and administrator to ensure a seamless client experience.
  • Coordinate with the fund’s Responsible Entity (RE) to resolve operational matters efficiently and maintain a strong Administrator–RE relationship.
  • Partner with the Australia Business Development team to understand priorities and support distribution initiatives.
  • Build and maintain a strong working relationship with the EMEA Operations team to ensure accurate trade processing.
  • Collaborate with the legal team to update documentation and ensure timely approval and implementation.
  • Work with the due diligence team to complete information requests promptly and accurately.
  • Develop and maintain quarterly KPIs for both external service providers and internal performance metrics.
  • Maintain a project tracker for open items and ensure timely updates across teams.
  • Prepare distribution updates and investor resolutions for international board materials.
  • Ensure all data providers and pricing sources receive accurate and up‑to‑date information.
  • Support leadership in managing service providers, distribution platforms, and other external vendors.
  • Collaborate across teams to produce reporting materials, guides, and resources that enhance operational efficiency.
  • Provide insights to refine and update marketing materials, and support proofreading of routine updates with strong attention to detail.
  • Assist and contribute to projects and analyses for the private wealth and business development teams.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Team player with a strong work ethic and an interest in private markets and/or private wealth.
  • Strong project management skills.
  • Ability to work independently while being a collaborative team player.
  • Highly organized and self-directed individual who pays close attention to detail and can multi-task among various projects.
  • Committed to meeting deadlines and progressing workstreams across multiple teams with limited supervision. 
  • Effective and clear communication skills, both verbally and written, with all levels of personnel, both internal and external.
  • Strong problem-solving capabilities with proven track record of leading projects and initiatives from beginning to end.
  • Highly motivated, ambitious individual with a growth mindset that flourishes in a challenging, fast-paced, and professional environment with frequent shifts of priorities as business needs dictate.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, business administration, management or business preferred
  • 5+ years of related financial services experience required
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Strong Excel skills and familiar working with Adobe Acrobat
  • Highly motivated individual with demonstrated initiative, independence and adaptability
  • Strong analytical and organizational skills and high attention to detail
  • Proven customer service skills

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Acute attention to accuracy and detail
  • Effective communication skills and strong problem-solving skills
  • Ability to prioritize activities while working on multiple projects and achieve target deadlines
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and SharePoint

  • Team oriented yet self-driven

jobs.description:

Position Overview:

The Senior Fund Accountant will support the day-to-day fund accounting, valuation, analysis and oversight of US and non-US Funds. The Senior Fund Accountant will work closely with StepStone Private Wealth Fund Accounting and StepStone Group Monitoring and Reporting teams.

Essential Job Functions:

This hands-on, detail-oriented position will:

  • Track daily investment activity, including funding investments and overseeing investment related expenses
  • Managing investment portfolio forecasts, GAAP, accruals and comparison to actuals
  • Provide assistance with updates to portfolio management data (SPI) based on daily reconciliations
  • Assist in the preparation of monthly Valuation Committee materials
  • Oversee communications with deal teams regarding investment valuations and GP updates
  • Complete operational due diligence on each GP/investment
  • Assist in the preparation and sign-off of net asset values
  • Prepare workpapers and supporting documentation for the annual financial statement audit
  • Support the accounting team in the closing of transactions, including responding to information requests, review of allocations, review of reporting requirements and other support and analysis as needed
  • Track daily cash activity, including prompting administrator wires and overseeing invoice/expense payments
  • Managing fund forecasts and comparing to actuals
  • Reconcile foreign exchange hedge positions for accuracy, timely break resolution, and compliance with fund accounting policies
  • Support various fund administrators and coordinate delivery of required reporting, etc.
  • Respond to sub-adviser and team member requests in a professional and timely manner
  • Assist with daily work associated with accounting and reporting functions
  • Assist with preparation of company dashboards and board decks
  • Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate

Qualifications:

  • Must possess a four-year college degree with a major in Accounting
  • Three to five years of accounting or audit experience
  • Previous experience with Investment Company Accounting and ASC 820 (FAS 157) preferred

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Strong Microsoft Excel experience
  • Ability to multi-task and manage priorities against key deadlines
  • Must be able to work independently, but contribute in a team-oriented environment
  • CPA preferred

jobs.title: 2025-2026 Private Equity Intern
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The team you’ll join

StepStone’s Private Equity (PE) team diligences and invests in opportunities across the private equity landscape. The team will invest directly in companies alongside other sponsors, complete LP and GP - led secondary transactions, and evaluate primary investments on behalf of StepStone’s advisory and discretionary clients. Through analysis of leveraged buyout deals and funds, StepStone’s PE team works across a wide array of industries and capital structures.

About the role

StepStone is currently seeking penultimate and final year undergraduate students or students enrolled in a master’s degree with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an intern position on StepStone’s Private Equity Team. StepStone offers Interns a unique opportunity to achieve a valuable learning experience working across the private equity industry. Through analysis of private equity funds and investments, Interns gain exposure to a wide array of industries and capital structures, and become accustomed to a variety of private equity funds and investment strategies.

What you’ll do

Interns will work in a team environment to target and perform due diligence, including financial modelling and preparation of investment memorandums, on opportunities across the Private Equity team’s three investment verticals: private equity fund investments, co-investment transactions and secondaries transactions.

Key responsibilities

  • Analyze and conduct diligence on co-investment opportunities and secondary transactions
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Conduct due diligence on potential fund investments (team, strategy, performance), and synthesize findings in an investment memorandum to be presented at the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Speak with senior executives to gather insights regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

What we’re looking for

  • Pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree, expected to graduate in 2026 or later
  • Completed at least one relevant 3- or 6-month internship, and able to work on-site at StepStone’s Beijing office at least four days per week 
  • High proficiency in MS Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Flexibility-StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Foreign language skills (desirable)

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in Private Equity.

jobs.title: 2026-2027 Private Equity Infrastructure & Real Assets Co-op Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The team you’ll join

StepStone Infrastructure and Real Assets (SIRA), established in 2014, is part of StepStone Group, specializing in private market investments. In 2024, SIRA managed US$103 billion in infrastructure and real assets, advising on or committing over US$11 billion across primaries, secondaries, and co-investments. The team includes 12 partners with over 20 years of experience on average and over 75 professionals, providing expertise in infrastructure, agriculture, and natural resources across Australia, Europe, and North America.

About the role

StepStone seeks candidates with strong quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills for a co-op opportunity with its Infrastructure and Real Assets Team. This role offers you valuable experience in private equity infrastructure. As a Co-op Analyst, you will gain exposure to diverse companies, assets, capital structures, and become acquainted with various infrastructure funds, managers, and transactions.

What you’ll do

As a Co-op Analyst, you will work in a team to conduct due diligence on SIRA’s investment opportunities in infrastructure funds, co-investments, and secondary transactions. You will analyze sectors like transportation, telecommunications, utilities, power, energy, logistics, renewables, social impact, and sustainability. You'll also prepare and present recommendations to SIRA’s Investment Committee.

Key responsibilities

  • For co-investments transactions:
    • Conduct market research to identify and analyze trends across various sectors using public information and databases
    • Form independent opinions on sector or investment attractiveness by analyzing data creatively; articulate findings clearly
    • Prepare valuation analyses of private companies or assets using methods like discounted cash flow and transaction comparables
    • Assist in creating and adjusting Excel-based financial models for investments
    • Construct and efficiently communicate investment recommendations in MS Word and PowerPoint
  • For fund investments and secondary transactions:
    • Review financial analyses from target fund managers, including investment track records and financial statement analysis
    • Support the development and adjustment of Excel-based financial models related to funds
    • Evaluate marketing and supporting materials of prospective fund managers to identify key value drivers and areas for further investigation
  • General research and asset management:
    • Assist senior researchers in managing and tracking investment opportunities
    • Evaluate and monitor current fund managers and portfolio company performance; attend meetings with target fund managers' executives
  • Internship project

What we’re looking for

  • Must be able to work legally in Canada without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • Must be able to work full-time hours, in-person in our Toronto office starting as early as July 2026 for 6-8 months
  • Undergraduate, Masters or MBA student graduating anywhere from September 2026 through June 2028
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Collaborative mindset with a proactive and team-oriented approach

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This winter internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in private markets.

Salary: 95,000 CAD annually

The team you’ll join

StepStone Real Estate (SRE) boasts extensive knowledge and experience with $163B in AUA and $19B in AUM as of 2024. SRE leverages the strengths of our investment and advisory teams, drawing on our deep market research and investment analysis to create compelling opportunities and tailored portfolios of fund investments, secondaries, and co-investments for institutional investors worldwide.

The Investment Team invests discretionary capital through our flagship GP-led secondaries fund, SREP, and several separately managed accounts (SMAs). The advisory team offers a full suite of capabilities to serve institutional investors seeking access to the private real estate markets on scale through committing to funds.   

About the role

As a Real Estate Intern, you will join StepStone’s Real Estate Team in Beijing and gain exposure to the dynamic world of private real estate investing. This internship is designed to provide a comprehensive learning experience, offering insight into market trends, investment strategies, and client advisory processes across the Asia Pacific region. You’ll work alongside experienced professionals in a collaborative environment that emphasizes mentorship and professional development.

What you’ll do

As a Real Estate Intern, you will contribute to projects that impact our investment and advisory processes. You’ll collaborate with team members, participate in due diligence, and develop insights that shape real estate strategies across global markets.

Key responsibilities

  • Assist in underwriting and assessing prospective fund managers for advisory mandates
  • Support evaluation of potential investments and asset management of the existing real estate portfolio
  • Perform market research using public sources and proprietary databases to identify and analyze macroeconomic trends
  • Prepare for and participate in meetings with fund managers and contribute to active investment and advisory due diligence
  • Create and present materials for internal discussions, including Investment Committee meetings
  • Communicate positions clearly in both written and verbal formats within a collaborative team environment

What we’re looking for

  • Must be able to work legally in China without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • Currently enrolled undergraduate (Junior or Senior) or Master’s student in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related field
  • Available to start in September 2026 and commit to a full-time internship for a minimum of three months (ideally 3–6 months)
  • Must maintain active student status throughout the internship period
  • Ability to work in-person at StepStone’s Beijing office
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word
  • Demonstrated record of strong academic performance
  • Team-oriented, highly motivated, and detail-oriented with a strong work ethic
  • Demonstrated interest in the investment industry; Real Estate exposure or interest is a plus

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in private equity real estate.

jobs.title: 2027 Private Equity Infrastructure & Real Assets Summer Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Application deadline: Rolling basis

The team you’ll join

StepStone Infrastructure and Real Assets (SIRA), established in 2014, is part of StepStone Group, specializing in private market investments across infrastructure and real assets. As of December 31, 2025, SIRA had responsibility for approximately US$132 billion in capital, deploying an average of over US$13 billion annually across primaries, secondaries, and co-investments. The global team comprises more than 75 investment professionals, offering deep expertise across infrastructure and real assets sub-sectors, including energy, transport, natural capital, agriculture, and other essential assets, with a presence across North America, Europe, and Australia.

About the role

StepStone seeks motivated undergraduates graduating between December 2027 and June 2028 to join our SIRA team as Summer Analysts. This role offers a valuable opportunity to gain insights into the private equity industry. As a Summer Analyst, you will learn about various sectors such as transportation, telecommunications, utilities, power, energy, logistics, renewables, social projects, sustainability, and impact investing. Additionally, you will be exposed to diverse companies, assets, and capital structures, providing you with a comprehensive understanding of infrastructure investments.

What you’ll do

As a Summer Analyst, you’ll work collaboratively to conduct due diligence on opportunities within SIRA's investment verticals, including infrastructure fund investments, co-investments, and secondaries. You will also prepare and present recommendations with the investment team for SIRA's Investment Committee.

Key responsibilities

  • Conduct thorough analysis and due diligence on co-investment opportunities, as well as primary and secondary transactions in private equity, specifically within the infrastructure, agriculture, and natural resources sectors
  • Perform financial modeling and analytical tasks including creating or modifying financial models, analyzing financial statements and forecasts, preparing valuation bridges, running discounted cash flow analyses, comparing public companies, and conducting sensitivity/scenario analyses
  • Develop and present a well-constructed argument regarding investment decisions, effectively communicating these arguments in written materials such as Microsoft Word documents and PowerPoint presentations
  • Interpret financial analyses prepared by target fund managers, including assessment of investment track records and financial statements; conduct valuation analyses on private companies or privately-owned assets using various methods, including discounted cash flow and transaction comparables
  • Perform market research utilizing publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify, and analyze trends across various sectors

What we’re looking for

  • Must be able to work legally in the U.S. without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • Undergraduate student graduating anywhere from December 2027 through June 2028
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Collaborative mindset with a proactive and team-oriented approach

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in private markets.
