  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

Your Team
You will join a global team of 27+ experienced portfolio managers from diverse backgrounds managing a large number of Equity and Fixed Income ETFs. The newly established Taiwan ETF PM team will play an integral part in managing and growing the local ETF business.

About the Role:

  • Manage passive ETFs, index funds, and feeder ETFs in equity, fixed income, or balanced/multi-asset categories.
  • Ensure compliance with Taiwan regulatory requirements and internal investment processes.
  • Oversee index reconstitution, portfolio trading, cash management, and corporate action decisions.
  • Monitor tracking error and implement strategies to optimize portfolio performance.
  • Collaborate with equity and fixed income analysts and traders to implement portfolio decisions.
  • Lead or contribute to investment process improvements to enhance efficiency and scalability.
  • Engage with internal stakeholders (capital markets, product development) and external clients (institutional due diligence, conferences).
  • Utilize advanced portfolio analytics and modeling tools (e.g., Aladdin, Bloomberg, FactSet, Barra/Axioma) for portfolio optimization.
  • Maintain thorough documentation of policies, procedures, and investment decisions.

Requirement for the Role :

  • Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field preferred.
  • CFA designation or progress towards CFA is preferred but not mandatory.
  • 5 to 15 years of asset management experience, specifically managing passive ETFs or mutual funds in both equity and fixed income.
  • Minimum 5 years of direct experience managing or co-managing passive funds/ETFs.
  • Strong knowledge of global equity and fixed income markets, index construction, and passive investment strategies.
  • Experience with portfolio optimization, tracking error management, and corporate action analysis.
  • Excellent communication skills for client engagement and cross-team collaboration.
  • Experience with portfolio management systems such as Aladdin, Bloomberg, or Charles River is preferred.
  • Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, SQL/Oracle, and VBA is a significant advantage.

  • On-site presence in Taipei office is mandatory.

  • Ability to work under regulatory requirements, personally implementing investment processes.

    Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment with portfolio managers, analysts, traders, compliance, and operations.

    Speak fluent English to communicate with the global team

Taiwan Licensing and Certification Requirements

The candidate must meet one of the following certification and experience criteria:

  • Certified Securities Investment Analyst (CSIA) License
  • Passed a Senior Securities Specialist Examination
  • Passed examination for Securities Investment Trust and Consulting Professionals

How to Apply:

  • Apply for the role directly here or at Invesco Careers: Careers at Invesco | APAC.

Employee

Yes

Job Description

About the Team / Department:
The ETF Product Strategy Group within the Americas Product is committed to providing superior client support and actionable investment insights that drive our product line’s strategic agenda for growth.

About the Role:

This individual will work closely with others on the team and help develop ETF research and analysis that positions our US ETFs in the current macro environment. The Sr. Analyst will work cross-functionally with Product Development, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Work alongside analysts and strategists on the team to develop product segment research and analysis on Invesco ETFs, the ETF industry, and broader financial markets serving as a subject matter expert for the product line
  • Develop and update product materials that position Invesco’s ETFs
  • Project manage and lead research projects as assigned by senior leaders
  • Execute on actions needed to maintain and improve product line
  • Partner closely with ETF strategists and specialists to support our clients and ensure product integrity, occasionally participating in client meetings

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree in business, finance, mathematics or related discipline required
  • 3-5 years of investment management and/or research experience preferred
  • Series 7 & 63 (already licensed is a plus, can study for these exams after being hired)
  • CFA track or progress towards advanced strongly preferred
  • Skills and professional development in a senior analyst role with a desire to take on more responsibility and accountability
  • Previous experience or solid understanding of factor, thematic or fixed-income investing strongly preferred
  • ETF and index familiarity and industry experience required
  • Experience with Bloomberg, Morningstar Direct, FactSet, Python a plus
  • Strong research and analytical skills to analyze and interpret various market research reports and data and apply such information to daily activities
  • Strong subject matter acumen and written communication skills
  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills; maintain effective, cooperative working relationships with all departments and organizational levels
  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives, then working independently to achieve them, seeking the team’s input as needed

jobs.title: Portfolio Manager, Agency MBS
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Structured Investments team manages a wide range of strategies to support the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform. Its mandate spans the residential and commercial agency and non-agency mortgage-backed sectors as well as the asset-backed securities sector with the goal of delivering best-in-class fund performance. The team manages approximately $44 bn USD across a variety of strategies including mutual funds, ETFs, institutional separately managed accounts, and an Agency-focused mortgage REIT.

The Portfolio Manager will serve as a key member of the team responsible for evaluating, selecting and positioning Agency MBS and related investments across Invesco-managed products.

Responsibilities:

  • Apply Agency MBS expertise to generate and implement investment ideas through comprehensive analysis of specified pools, CMOs, TBAs, and other agency mortgage derivatives

  • Manage portfolios focused primarily on Agency MBS across various investment vehicles, including mutual funds, ETFs and separately managed institutional accounts

  • Collaborate with portfolio management colleagues on investment strategies managed by the broader Structured Investments and Invesco Fixed Income team

  • Employ a relative value-oriented mindset when assessing cross-sector asset allocation opportunities across the universe of structured products and other investment grade credit sectors

  • Add value through design and execution of advanced hedging strategies

  • Develop and communicate investment outlook to internal and external stakeholders

  • Build trusted relationships with sell-side counterparties to facilitate best execution on Agency MBS sourcing and trading

  • Cultivate strong, lasting partnerships with current and prospective retail and institutional clients

  • Commercialize Agency MBS and broader structured products investment capabilities through product development and marketing efforts

Qualifications:

  • 10+ years of direct experience in analyzing, trading, and managing risk in Agency MBS including TBA, specified pools, CMOs, and derivatives

  • CFA or MBA preferred

  • Comprehensive understanding of all facets of Agency MBS and related macroeconomic, financial market, and regulatory dynamics

  • Significant knowledge of other structured products sectors

  • Expertise in design and implementation of hedging strategies via swaps, futures, swaptions, and other interest rate derivatives

  • Experience managing levered portfolios is beneficial

  • Command of industry tools including Bloomberg, Tradeweb, Yield Book, DataQuery, etc.

  • Familiarity with BlackRock Aladdin platform is beneficial

Preferred Skills & Attributes:

  • Demonstrated success in driving investment outcomes through decisive risk-taking and portfolio management

  • Proficiency in quantitative methods and analytical mindset

  • Ability to articulate a comprehensive investment framework and adapt it to meet specific client objectives

  • Humble, client centric focus - we are fiduciaries of our clients’ assets and must understand their goals, desires, and investment objectives

  • Commercially oriented mindset

  • Strong written and verbal presentation skills and ability to work constructively as a key member of a dynamic investment team

  • Proficiency leveraging AI tools to automate and optimize workflows

jobs.title: Senior Principal, Bank Loan Securities
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

Your Role
The Senior Principal will serve as a highly experienced subject matter expert in Bank Loan and Private Credit markets, providing strategic leadership and technical expertise across large-scale transformation initiatives. This role is a senior individual contributor position requiring deep knowledge of bank loan instruments, market infrastructure, and regulatory frameworks. The Senior Principal will drive operational excellence, process optimization, and best practice adoption while collaborating with internal and external stakeholders to ensure superior service delivery and risk mitigation.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Lead and influence large-scale transformation projects within Bank Loan Services, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and industry best practices.

  • Develop and implement Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to monitor operational health and performance.

  • Champion continuous improvement initiatives and automation strategies to enhance efficiency and reduce operational risk.

  • Maintain deep understanding of Private Credit and Bank Loan markets, including CLO structures, trade settlement, and lifecycle management.

  • Act as a senior escalation point for complex issues such as restructurings, corporate actions, and new fund launches.

  • Oversee communications and coordination with trustees, agent banks, custodians, and other third-party providers.

  • Ensure robust internal controls and compliance with regulatory requirements.

  • Conduct process reviews and recommend enhancements to achieve best practice standards.

  • Design and implement solutions for new products in collaboration with FO, MO, technology teams and other stakeholders as necessary.

  • Lead audit processes across multiple fund structures with internal and external auditors.

  • Serve as a trusted advisor and mentor, providing training and guidance to team members and leadership as needed.

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders (Front Office, MO, Global Operations, Risk, Legal, Fund Accounting) and external partners.

  • Represent Bank Loan Services in committees, service meetings, and industry forums.


The Experience You Bring:

  • Minimum 15 years of experience in investment industry with a focus on Private Credit and Bank Loan markets.

  • Proven track record of leading complex projects and transformation initiatives.

  • Extensive knowledge of trustee, agent bank, and custodian operations.

  • Familiarity with Wall Street Office Suite, Everest, and S&P Global industry tools/ apps, and rating agency platforms (S&P, Moody’s).

  • Strong understanding of automation tools and data analytics for process improvement.

  • Exceptional leadership and strategic thinking skills.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for engaging stakeholders at all levels.

  • BA/BS – Finance/Accounting preferred

#LI-SD1

The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $160,000 - $185,000/year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

Your Team

The ETF Product Management group is tasked with the development and execution of category and product specific strategies and tactics designed to better position the Invesco ETF product line in the competitive exchange traded product (ETP) market. 


Your Role

The Senior Fixed Income Product Strategist will leverage his/her capital markets, investment management and product knowledge to deliver market views and product positioning to institutions and financial advisors and will develop timely market outlook and sales ideas to address changing market conditions. This individual, in working with the Head of Fixed Income ETF Product Strategy, as part of the Product Strategy and Research Team, will be an expert in the assigned products and categories, and is responsible for supporting the research and strategy content development for the assigned product suite. The individual will utilize a client-focused approach to help communicate product strategy to effectively position and market assigned products.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Drive growth of the Fixed Income ETF product line in partnership with cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, capital markets, product management, portfolio management and product development) 

  • Help build and reinforce a brand for Invesco products by presenting at industry conferences and serving as the “voice” for the Fixed Income ETF product line in media interviews, sponsored events, webcasts, etc.

  • Support efforts to develop and manage insights on the product line through

  • Asset class and product-specific communication

  • Product training and positioning

  • Product-specific competitive analysis

  • Partner with Sales to execute product-specific distribution strategies and participate in client meetings

  • Content development in partnership with Marketing

  • Market and product performance commentaries

  • Monitor macro market conditions to formulate asset class views, and translate them into actionable investment and product positioning ideas for clients

  • Identify new product opportunities as well as assess existing product line to identify opportunities for enhancement

  • Help manage team responsible for Invesco Fixed Income ETF Strategy


The Experience You Bring:

  • 10 years of investment management and/or research experience required

  • Previous experience and solid understanding of fixed-income markets required

  • ETF and/or index familiarity and industry experience required

jobs.title: Associate, Private Equity Advisory
jobs.description:
About Kingfish Group
Kingfish is a different kind of private equity firm. We have pioneered a new approach to private equity investing that places us at the intersection of people, ideas and capital. At the core of our efforts are deep, systematic relationships with proven operating executives. Our firm comprises two core business segments; our private equity investment business and an advisory business that supports a limited set of high-performing PE sponsors as they identify, invest in and grow businesses. Our philosophy is to find the best people and give them the freedom to learn, innovate and outperform. In that spirit, we are structured to ensure that each individual contributes substantially to firm strategy and goals, and there is extensive feedback on performance. We pride ourselves on assembling high-performing teams to pursue inspiring goals.
Position Summary
Our Associates work closely with our private equity firm partners, operating as an extension of their deal teams to evaluate investment opportunities and advance portfolio company initiatives. Specifically, Associates are responsible for (a) Building relationships with industry executives and aligning them to support the team throughout the investment process (b) Synthesizing information and translating it into key insights that support investment decision making, (c) Managing projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships that provide an advantage to the team (d) Leading company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Responsibilities
● Build relationships with industry executives and align them to support the team throughout the investment process
  ○ Guide our research team as they identify industry executives and other primary information relevant for a particular investment opportunity
  ○ Lead calls with industry executives, serving as the primary point of contact and ambassador for the firm
●  Synthesize information and translate into key insights that support investment decision making
  ○  Analyze industries, markets and competitive landscapes to better understand investment opportunities
  ○  Perform primary diligence as needed alongside executive advisors throughout the deal process
  ○  Identify key insights from information and recommendations gathered from conversations with industry executives and research
●   Manage projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships, providing an advantage to the team
  ○  Manage project plans and deal timelines, working closely with senior leaders at our private equity partners to ensure our work is focused on the highest priorities and maximum impact
  ○  Create and deliver high quality presentations and memoranda for our Partners as well as Kingfish leadership (e.g. primary diligence deliverables, executive spec documentation, and Kingfish Investment Committee discussions)
●   Lead company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Qualifications
● Bachelor’s degree with 2-3 years of experience within consulting or financial services
● Relationship Building: High EQ with advanced written/ verbal communication skills, executive presence and an ability to build relationships with stakeholders at all levels
● Intellectual Curiosity & Creativity: thoughtful in asking questions and pursuing answers, excited to learn
● Critical Thinking: breaks down complex problems and solves them in creative ways
● Communication & Information Synthesis: effectively identifies key insights and presents insights
● Project Management: ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with tight timelines
● Proactive & Collaborative: team player that takes initiative to improve the team and the firm

jobs.title: Associate, Investor Relations, Private Credit
jobs.description:
Our Company

Oaktree is a leader among global investment managers specializing in alternative investments, with more than $220 billion in assets under management. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

We are committed to cultivating an environment that is collaborative, curious, inclusive and honors diversity of thought. Providing training and career development opportunities and emphasizing strong support for our local communities through philanthropic initiatives are essential to our culture.

Investor Relations is a part of Oaktree’s investment teams with responsibility for growing and deepening relationships with the clients and prospects investing in our respective strategies. Investor Relations professionals serve as representatives and subject matter experts for Oaktree’s strategies and products, partnering closely with our sales and fundraise management teams to drive growth and deepen client engagement.

For additional information, please visit Oaktree’s website at http://www.oaktreecapital.com/

Responsibilities

This role will be aligned to Oaktree’s corporate direct lending and multi-strategy private credit strategies, supporting the execution of investor communication and materials for the relevant strategies.  This individual will work closely with other investor relations team members, investment professionals, our Global Client Group, and other internal stakeholders to deliver high-quality investor materials, support client engagement efforts, and ensure timely and accurate responses to investor inquiries.  The role offers the opportunity to develop deep knowledge of leveraged finance and private credit strategies within a collaborative, investment-focused environment.

Primary responsibilities would include:

Investor Communications

  • Support preparation for client meetings, including identifying materials to use, ensuring data accuracy and consistency across materials, and coordinating inputs across teams
  • Prepare responses to regular and ad-hoc investor inquiries relating to existing portfolios’ investments and overall positioning
  • Help coordinate materials for periodic client engagement such as LPAC meetings or co-investment opportunities
  • Contribute to fund brochures, talking points on salient topics, and other marketing and client-facing materials

Project Management & Execution

  • Contribute to a high-performing investor relations team by maintaining strong attention to detail and execution quality
  • Track deliverables and timelines to ensure timely reply to clients and timely completion of materials
  • Identify required information and coordinate across investment team, reporting teams, Global Client Group and other stakeholders to collate required information and present it to investors
  • Take ownership of the quality of the end-product, even when multiple teams have contributed
  • Demonstrate a proactive approach to problem-solving

Insight Development

  • Develop a strong understanding of portfolio holdings, strategy positioning, and market dynamics
  • Support the team in synthesizing investment insights into clear, client-ready materials
  • Contribute to written commentary regarding the portfolios, building the foundation to participate more actively in client discussions over time
  • Develop sound judgment with respect to sensitive, client-facing matters


Qualifications

  • 2-3 years of relevant experience in a client-facing role in institutional asset management 
  • Strong interest in markets and leveraged finance, with an ability to communicate effectively (written and verbal)
  • Ability to exercise good judgment regarding investor requests
  • Ability to interact with clients, investment teams and investors
  • Strong project management and teamwork skills
  • FINRA licenses (Series 7 and 63) or the ability to obtain them within a compressed timeframe
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word), with the ability to create both client-facing and internal deliverables

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree (major in Finance, Business, Marketing, Communications, or related field preferred).
  • MBA, CFA or other recognized advanced credentials is a plus.

Base Salary Range

$125,000 - $155,000

In addition to a competitive base salary, you will be eligible to receive discretionary bonus incentives, a comprehensive benefits package and a flexible work arrangement.  The base salary offered will be commensurate with experience and/or qualifications, industry knowledge and expertise, as well as prior training and education.

Equal Opportunity Employment Policy

Oaktree is committed to diversity and to equal opportunity employment. Oaktree does not make employment decisions on the basis of race, creed, color, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, past or present physical or mental disability, HIV status, medical condition as defined by state law (genetic characteristics or cancer), pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran status, military service, marital status, familial status, genetic information, domestic violence victim status or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state and local laws and ordinances. This policy applies to hiring, placement, internal promotions, training, opportunities for advancement, recruitment advertising, transfers, demotions, layoffs, terminations, recruitment advertising, rates of pay and other forms of compensation and all other terms, conditions and privileges of employment. This policy applies to all Oaktree applicants, employees, clients, and contractors. Staff members wishing to report violations or suspected violations of this policy should contact the head of their department or Human Resources.

For positions based in Los Angeles

For those applying for a position in the city of Los Angeles, the firm will consider for employment qualified applicants with a criminal history in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

jobs.title: Vice President, Global Opportunities - Healthcare
jobs.description:
Our Company

Oaktree is a leader among global investment managers specializing in alternative investments, with more than $220 billion in assets under management. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

We are committed to cultivating an environment that is collaborative, curious, inclusive and honors diversity of thought. Providing training and career development opportunities and emphasizing strong support for our local communities through philanthropic initiatives are essential to our culture.

Launched in 1988, Global Opportunities, Oaktree’s flagship opportunistic credit strategy, is currently investing out of its $16 billion Fund XII. It employs an all-weather strategy with a broad mandate, and ability to invest across the capital structure in debt or equity with a focus on finding the most attractive public and private opportunities worldwide. The strategy (previously known as Distressed Opportunities) has deep roots in distressed debt, reorganizations, and restructurings, but it has evolved over the last thirty years to include a wide-ranging set of investment activities targeting a mid-teens and above return. These include providing proprietarily originated capital solutions in structured and risk-controlled ways to borrowers with limited access to funding; buying assets in out-of-favor industries; and creating platforms with superior management teams who seek to exploit attractive undervalued, niche, and/or dislocation-driven opportunities.

For additional information, please visit Oaktree’s website at http://www.oaktreecapital.com/

Responsibilities

Oaktree is seeking a Vice President to work within the Opportunities Funds in either the Los Angeles or New York office. The Opportunities Funds specialize in distressed, value-based investing but have a very broad mandate and can invest across the capital structure and in various asset classes, industries and geographies. The role will focus on healthcare coverage across sub-industries and asset classes within the sector. Healthcare is one of the most active industries across the Opportunities Funds portfolio with a strong track record of leading returns. 

The Vice President will participate in all investment functions and portfolio management activities including the following:

  • Preparing detailed financial modeling;
  • Performing quantitative and qualitative investment analysis; 
  • Developing and enhancing business and industry due diligence across healthcare, including idea generation, sourcing and industry relationship development;
  • Participating in deal structuring, legal documentation and negotiations; and
  • Monitoring the performance of existing portfolio investments.

Qualifications

  • Undergraduate degree with an excellent track record of academic achievement
  • 4+ years of investment experience with a top-tier investment firm (ie opportunistic alternative asset manager, private credit, private equity, credit-oriented hedge fund) or investment bank.
  • Exceptional skills in analyzing and presenting investment opportunities, including: business and industry analysis, company valuation, credit analysis, negotiations and structuring.
  • Strong quantitative capabilities in data analysis, financial modeling and presentation of analysis.

Personal Attributes

  • Demonstrates self-initiative and a strong work ethic;
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills;
  • Strong interpersonal skills and a sense of self awareness;
  • High degree of precision in work / attention to detail;
  • Strong problem-solving skills and business acumen; and
  • Team-oriented with strong integrity and professionalism.

Education

Bachelor’s degree from a top tier undergrad and / or MBA degree from a top tier business school

Base Salary Range

$275,000 - $300,000

In addition to a competitive base salary, you will be eligible to receive discretionary bonus incentives, a comprehensive benefits package and a flexible work arrangement.  The base salary offered will be commensurate with experience and/or qualifications, industry knowledge and expertise, as well as prior training and education.

This policy applies to all Oaktree applicants, employees, clients, and contractors. jobs.title: Analyst/Associate, Investor Relations (Private Equity)
jobs.description:
Our Company

Oaktree is a leader among global investment managers specializing in alternative investments, with more than $220 billion in assets under management. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

We are committed to cultivating an environment that is collaborative, curious, inclusive and honors diversity of thought. Providing training and career development opportunities and emphasizing strong support for our local communities through philanthropic initiatives are essential to our culture.

Investor Relations is a part of Oaktree’s investment teams with responsibility for growing and deepening relationships with the clients and prospects investing in our respective strategies. Investor Relations professionals serve as representatives and subject matter experts for Oaktree’s strategies and products, partnering closely with our sales and fundraise management teams to drive growth and deepen client engagement.

For additional information, please visit Oaktree’s website at http://www.oaktreecapital.com/

Responsibilities

This role will be primarily aligned to Oaktree’s Special Situations and Power Opportunities strategies.  This individual will support these strategies at the direction and under supervision of our senior Investor Relations professionals.  The candidate will be expected to develop internal subject matter expertise regarding the strategies’ approach, areas of focus and portfolios along with industry regulations and firm standards.  This role is based in Los Angeles, CA.

Selected responsibilities include:

  • Support the senior Investor Relations professionals in the design, creation and management of client collateral for internal and external consumption, including marketing materials, performance/market commentary and other investor communications (e.g., presentations, factsheets, quarterly reports, talking points etc.)
  • Respond to or provide information on product- or strategy-specific inquiries and requests under the direction of senior Investor Relations professionals
    • Understand the strategy(ies) or product(s), market(s) and operational structure(s) to be able to draft responses to investor inquiries where appropriate
    • Coordinate with the appropriate internal resources when information is not readily available
  • Support technical product/investment-related investor requests; support the completion of due diligence questionnaires in partnership with Oaktree’s diligence and reporting team
  • Alongside the senior Investor Relations professionals, develop subject matter expertise for the relevant strategies, including: philosophy and approach, markets and areas of focus, competitors, portfolio composition and performance, sustainability integration, among others.
  • Assist with the preparation for product-related investor calls and meetings, including new business development pitches, portfolio reviews, webcasts, and live events
  • Assist in the execution of their respective strategy’s co-investment program, including preparing co-investment deal materials, curating prospective investor lists, and managing the investor due diligence process
  • Assist in coordination and preparation of materials for fund LPAC (i.e., limited partner advisory committee) meetings
  • Support the formation and oversight of separate accounts designed to achieve client needs and objectives
  • Develop internal materials or analysis to support new product ideas
  • Ensure all communications are fully compliant with industry regulations and firm standards

Qualifications

  • 1-3 years of relevant experience preferably in investment banking, institutional asset management, or consulting; prior client-facing experience is a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word), with the ability to create both client-facing and internal deliverables
  • Strong project management and teamwork skills, including the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work collaboratively within a team environment

Personal Attributes

  • Have the ability to quickly develop a deep understanding of the investment, legal, accounting, compliance, and other aspects of Oaktree’s product offerings
  • Exhibit outstanding attention to detail and exceptional organizational skills
  • Possess excellent interpersonal and communication skills (both verbal and written), with the ability to clearly and simply explain technical concepts 
  • Be a self-starter who proactively originates ideas, identifies and captures opportunities, and drives initiatives forward with limited oversight
  • Thrive in a dynamic, high-rigor environment where responsibilities may shift daily
  • Be highly resourceful, exercising sound judgment in responding to internal and investor requests by interpreting them accurately, asking thoughtful questions, seeking guidance, and escalating when necessary
  • Demonstrate poise, confidence, and professionalism to build relationships, gain support, and engage team members to ensure successful project execution
  • Be highly motivated to succeed and committed to supporting the success of teammates
  • Excel in a fast-paced, demanding environment while maintaining exceptional quality standards
  • Be a natural problem-solver who can independently identify and recommend improvements to drive productivity and efficiency
  • Demonstrate the ability to interact confidently with clients, investment professionals, and senior leadership
  • Uphold the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and dedication to excellence
  • Embrace diversity and uphold all core values of Oaktree

Education

Bachelor’s degree (major in Finance, Business, Economics, or related field preferred).

Base Salary Range

Analyst: $105,000 - $125,000

Associate: $125,000 - $155,000

In addition to a competitive base salary, you will be eligible to receive discretionary bonus incentives, a comprehensive benefits package and a flexible work arrangement.  The base salary offered will be commensurate with experience and/or qualifications, industry knowledge and expertise, as well as prior training and education.

Equal Opportunity Employment Policy

Oaktree is committed to diversity and to equal opportunity employment. Oaktree does not make employment decisions on the basis of race, creed, color, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, past or present physical or mental disability, HIV status, medical condition as defined by state law (genetic characteristics or cancer), pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran status, military service, marital status, familial status, genetic information, domestic violence victim status or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state and local laws and ordinances. This policy applies to hiring, placement, internal promotions, training, opportunities for advancement, recruitment advertising, transfers, demotions, layoffs, terminations, recruitment advertising, rates of pay and other forms of compensation and all other terms, conditions and privileges of employment. This policy applies to all Oaktree applicants, employees, clients, and contractors. Staff members wishing to report violations or suspected violations of this policy should contact the head of their department or Human Resources.

For positions based in Los Angeles

For those applying for a position in the city of Los Angeles, the firm will consider for employment qualified applicants with a criminal history in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

jobs.title: Investment Director, Infrastructure & Natural Resources
jobs.description:
Company: Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board

160 Front Street West, Suite 3200

Toronto, Ontario

M5J 0G4

Job Title: Investment Director, Infrastructure and Natural Resources – Latin America

NOC: 10021 – Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers

Wage: Starting at an annual base salary of $195,000-215,000 (37.5 hours per week) - permanent/full-time

Location(s) of work: 160 Front Street West, Suite 3200, Toronto, Ontario, M5J 0G4 Canada

Number of Positions: One (1)

Required Language Skills: English. Portuguese and/or Spanish an asset

The Opportunity

Infrastructure & Natural Resources (INR) is seeking an Investment Director to join our Americas team, with a coverage focus on Latin America. Based in Toronto, this role is particularly well-suited to highly driven professionals with significant direct equity investing and/or investment banking experience in infrastructure.

As an Investment Director, you will play a central role across the investment lifecycle -originating and executing new opportunities, while actively managing portfolio companies within the infrastructure sector. In this capacity, you will serve on boards and partner closely with management teams to shape strategy and drive value creation.

This role provides a platform to further deepen your investment expertise across Latin American markets and sectors, while continuing to build credibility and long-term relationships with management teams, partners, and industry participants.

Who you'll work with

Reporting to the Managing Director and Head of South America Infrastructure coverage, the Investment Director will be a key part of deal and asset management teams comprising investment analysts, investment associates, and investment principals, supported by third-party consultants, operating executives, and legal and accounting professionals dedicated to each particular portfolio company.

The broader INR team includes 70 investment professionals leading the execution of marquee infrastructure transactions and actively managing our portfolio companies. With offices in Toronto, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, INR manages a diverse portfolio of c. C$46.5 billion across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.

Key Responsibilities

The Investment Director will play an integral role in both the evaluation and execution of direct equity transactions, as well as in the ongoing management of existing portfolio companies. A key aspect of the role will also involve overseeing, providing guidance to, and mentoring junior team members.

Additionally, they will:

  • Actively manage INR portfolio companies, including serving on boards and partnering with management teams to shape strategy and drive value creation initiatives

  • Originate, evaluate, and pursue selective investment opportunities, with a particular focus on Latin America

  • Play a central role in all stages of equity investment execution, leading key workstreams, overseeing valuation and financial analysis, managing internal teams and external advisors, and making investment recommendations

  • Identify and diligence key business and investment transaction risks, and determine how best to mitigate them

  • Build and maintain relationships with management teams, directors, partners, lenders, advisors, and broader industry participants

  • Supervise and develop junior staff, providing clear direction and ongoing feedback

  • Represent OTPP at regional industry conferences and portfolio company meetings and events

Required Skills

  • 10+ years of professional experience in direct equity investing or investment banking, with a focus on infrastructure

  • 5+ years of investment coverage experience of Latin America is required

  • In-country experience in Brazil, Chile, and/or Mexico is an asset

  • Willingness and ability to travel frequently (20%+)

  • Strong ability to influence outcomes across a range of stakeholders, including management teams, boards, and partners

  • Sound commercial judgement and ability to form independent views and make decisions with imperfect information in a fast-paced environment

  • Strong financial acumen and quantitative skills, including experience overseeing valuation and financial analysis

  • Proven track record in leading the equity investment process, including due diligence, transaction execution, and debt financing

  • Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple workstreams and teams effectively under tight timelines

·        Fluency in English required. Portuguese and/or Spanish is an asset

Required Education

·        Undergraduate Degree or Master’s Degree in Economics, Commerce, Finance or Business Administration or a related field.

Benefits

  • Pay for performance environment that offers competitive salary and incentives

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage, including a Health Spending Account

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • Competitive time off

  • Discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

  • 20 vacation days

  • Bonus (Discretionary Pay for Performance)

  • Other Benefits (Employee Assistance Program)

  • Medical Benefits

  • Dental Benefits

  • Disability Benefits

  • Life Insurance Benefits

  • Group Insurance Benefits

  • Pension Benefits

  • Vision Care Benefits

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

jobs.title: Intern - Finance, Valuation, Risk Analytics (September 2026 - 4 or 8 month Contract)
jobs.description:
The deadline to apply for this role is:

Until 11:59 PM of May 25, 2026

The opportunity

September-December 2026 or September 2026-April 2027

Are you passionate about the future of Big Data in Finance?

If yes, we are the team for you. We work with a billion numbers a day which support the effective monitoring of the risk for the fund!

Finance is a diverse team of professionals with multifaceted backgrounds (CA, CFA, MBA, MMF). On the forefront of the investments industry, we are continually presented with new opportunities and challenges. We provide accounting and operational services and middle office analysis including performance measurement and risk modelling.

As a Risk Analytics Intern, you will be exposed to the risk measurement process from data inputs to risk analysis, which facilitates the daily monitoring of risk exposure of the OTPP investment book. In this role, you will gain knowledge in understanding drivers of market risk changes, investment product pricing model, improve skills in Bloomberg, SQL and VBA coding, and apply math/computer science/business knowledge in a hands-on environment!

Who you’ll work with

The Risk Analytic team is a fast paced and robust team that is responsible for producing risk reports in a timely and accurate manner to facilitate investment decision making. The Risk analytics team supports monitoring and reporting the fund’s overall market, and credit risk exposure and supports daily production data initiatives related to all input data and metrics.

As a Risk Analytics Intern, you will work in the Toronto office reporting to a Manager/Senior Associate and within a team of 10+ professionals. You will get the chance to collaborate closely with multiple partners, including Risk, Data Management, Model Vetting Group, Financial Operations, and IT support team.

What you’ll do

You will play a meaningful role in daily risk reporting while ensuring the integrity and quality of data used by the enterprise risk system. You will also be exposed to the risk calculation process and methodology of a leading-edge risk management department.

  • You will be performing analysis of risk factor’s impact on product/portfolio risk changes
  • Get to learn about risk calculation, valuation of various investment products and analyzing driving risk factors of those products.
  • Engage in “what-if” simulation process or risk impact analysis
  • Proposed and implement improvements to risk calculation operational process
  • You will be responsible to validate trade completeness, new trades, and perform market data quality checks on various data sources and programs

What you’ll need

  • Strong quantitative background in Mathematics, Statistics or Computer Science, along with a curiosity about Finance, Risk or Financial Engineering
  • Advanced knowledge of Excel and VBA
  • Experience with SQL
  • Experience using Python is an asset
  • Previous work experience in financial industry
  • Ability to work in a fast paced environment
  • Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
  • Experience with Bloomberg is an asset
  • An investment/risk management concentration is an asset
  • Must be enrolled in 3rd or 4th year of studies
  • You should be enrolled in a co-op program or returning back to your studies after the work term is completed

What we’re offering

The expected hourly range for this role is $24 - $28 depending on experience and qualifications.

jobs.title: Intern - Portfolio Engineering, Capital Markets, CMIA (September 2026 - 8 months)
jobs.description:
The deadline to apply for this role is:

Until 11:59 PM of May 24, 2026

The opportunity  

September 2026 - April 2027

Do you want to use your technical skills and curiosity about markets to work and build tools for a software-based trading portfolio? 

We are Capital Markets Investment Analytics (CMIA), an innovation-driven and centralized technology team within Capital Markets at Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan. We manage a broad and diversified set of portfolios and systematic applications. Development and maintenance of these applications require a unique combination of strong software skills and investment knowledge. We are looking for an intern to join our team this winter for an 8-month term.  

We are a team of software engineers that work closely with portfolio managers to help implement and support various portfolios and applications. Our applications consist of back-testing, execution, and attribution.  

Who you’ll work with  

CMIA is a team of 4, based in Toronto.  You will work alongside investment professionals in a world-class portfolio engineering team with a focus on investment-related software applications. We work very closely in an open and collaborative environment. We are entrepreneurial, tight knit, and have a performance driven culture. You will be mentored and guided by senior members of the CMIA team.  

What you’ll do 

  • Improve and work on our internal proprietary investment strategy codebase under the guidance of senior colleagues in CMIA. 

  • The code you will work on directly supports investment functions including but not limited to: proprietary investment strategies, automated algorithmic trading, portfolio management, order management, risk analysis, automated reporting, profit and loss analysis, performance attribution, portfolio analytics, quantitative strategies research & implementation, regression tests, investment reporting. 

  • Proactively look for areas of improvement and bring new ideas to the team through analysis and research. 

  • Investigate and attribute portfolio profit and loss to various strategies and factors. 

  • Work on a variety of datasets (e.g., sourcing, cleaning, visualization). 

  • Gain experience in one or several asset classes, including equities, fixed income, currencies, and commodities. 

  • Contribute to the development and optimization of our portfolio implementation infrastructure and analytical tools. 

What you’ll need 

  • To be enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program in quantitative discipline such as Engineering or Computer Science.  

  • Solid understanding of software engineering concepts and techniques, including design patterns, object-oriented programming, database, and version control systems. 

  • Strong interest in financial markets. 

  • Strong motivation to learn about systematic investment strategies and automated trading systems. 

  • Strong programming skills in a variety of modern programming languages, with a preference for Python, MATLAB, and JavaScript. 

  • Highly creative, entrepreneurial, and resourceful. 

  • You should be returning back to your studies after the work term is completed

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Risk - Models (Private Markets)
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Investment Associate, Investment Risk, Private Models is responsible for a variety of research and development activities primarily for the purpose of developing and distributing production grade risk models for various private assets invested by OTPP’s private programs in Equities, Real Estate, Infrastructure, Natural Resources, and Teachers’ Venture Growth. We are looking to add a creative and lateral thinker to supplement our activities and provide value-add to the Private Models team and the OTPP Board.

Who you'll work with

Reporting to the Director, you will work with dedicated team members to measure and report the market and credit risks of our private holdings in Equities, Real Estate, Infrastructure, Natural Resources, and Teachers’ Venture Growth. We model, monitor, and report investment risks for private assets of over $140 billion.

Working in the Private Models team is an exciting opportunity that is challenging and complex, requiring a high degree of ingenuity, perseverance and commitment to excellence. Members of the team are supported by senior team members to develop their skills and knowledge, but high levels of initiative and creativity are expected.

The risk models developed impact the Plan’s investment decision-making, performance and risk measurement. Models are reviewed by senior team members and then validated by the Model Validation Group.

What you'll do

  • Research and develop risk models for various private asset classes and individual investments in private markets.

  • Contribute to the creation, maintenance and improvement of existing asset-specific risk models, including (but not limited to) market and credit risk.

  • Write detailed documentation of methodology, validation testing and data specifications used by model.

  • Work with BA, IT, Data Management, Risk Analytics and the Model Validation Group to ensure model integration and validation are completed in a timely manner.

  • Conduct model recalibration on an annual basis to keep the models up-to-date.

  • Conduct risk assessment and provide risk insights for sizable potential new private deals for deal teams to present to Investment Committee on a bi-weekly basis.

  • Work cross-functionally with other teams and departments to develop models/tools used for investment decision-making. This includes supporting and sharing knowledge within Investment Risk and responding to ad hoc requests from investment departments.

  • Proactively explores opportunities to leverage AI and emerging technologies to enhance modelling capabilities, improve efficiency and generate deeper insights.

What you'll need

  • Masters’ degree in a quantitative discipline (MMF, MFE, Computational Finance, MQF) in addition to an undergraduate degree in a related field.

  • Min. 2 years’ experience in quantitative finance and/or risk management.

  • Knowledge/experience in private assets risk modeling (or related) is an asset.

  • Strong programming skills in SQL/MATLAB/VBA a definite asset.

  • Knowledge/experience in AI applications (ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude) is an asset

  • Expert proficiency in MS Office applications (with emphasis on Excel).

  • Strong conceptual thinker and problem solver, ability to research and test out new ideas/concepts to bridge the gap between theory and practice.

  • Excellent interpersonal, communication, and teamwork skills.

  • Attention to detail and precision is critical.

  • Successful completion of the FRM and CFA designation is an asset.

Pay-for-performance with base salary and annual incentive based on individual and enterprise performance. The Base Salary range for this level is as follows: $105,000 - $155,000. Individual compensation and placement of this role in the range is based on different factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, demonstrated competencies, and internal equity as they relate to the role.

jobs.title: Manager, Due Diligence (12 month contract)
jobs.description:
Manager, Due Diligence (12 month contract)

The Opportunity

The role of Manager, Due Diligence is to protect OTPP’s assets and reputation by informing investment decision making through identification of operational and business risks in OTPP’s externally managed investments; and advocating for mitigants to those risks.

This role supports the planning and execution of operational due diligence (ODD) reviews of external managers and key service providers in accordance with the OTPP’s External Manager Due Diligence Policy and requires domestic and international travel, including Europe and Asia.

Who you'll work with

You will frequently interface with senior management, executives, internal and external stakeholders. The nature of the relationship with these parties requires a high degree of professionalism, diplomacy, influence, and the ability to defend one’s position. 

What you'll do

ODD Reviews:

  • Support senior team members in performing operational risk assessments to be delivered to internal stakeholders including the Director and MD of the Due Diligence & Advisory, as well as the CFO, Investment teams, Legal and other stakeholders as required.

  • For each external manager, develop an understanding of its internal controls; deal / trade process flows; fund transfer, settlement, custody, and valuation processes; performance and risk measurement activities; regulatory and compliance environment; information technology systems and capabilities, cybersecurity programs, as well as business continuity and disaster recovery procedures.

Stakeholder Engagement:

  • Provide exceptional service to the Investment departments by proactively addressing concerns / issues and responding to inquiries and requests; and work with various internal departments to ensure the efficient and effective use of resources.

  • Assist senior team members in developing strong business relationships with internal stakeholders, as well as external partners and advisors to support and deliver upon Teachers’ Investment objectives.

Best Practices:

  • Support the adoption of best practices within the team’s activities to address emerging risks, new investment strategies, and other material developments (including the review and update of relevant policies and procedures as necessary)

Management accountability

  • Contribute to team effectiveness by sharing knowledge, supporting colleagues, and maintaining quality standards for ODD deliverables

  • Represent the Due Diligence group professionally in interactions with internal stakeholders and external partners.

  • Participate in team planning discussions in establishing performance goals, and provide input on process improvements and resource needs.

  • Demonstrate OTPP’s values consistently in interactions across all levels of the organization and with external partners.

What you'll need

  • Relevant university degree(s) – Finance, Accounting, Commerce

  • A master’s degree in finance and/or accounting, CPA, CFA, CAIA, or other equivalent international accreditation/designation is an asset

  • Strong knowledge and understanding of different public and private market investment strategies, public market infrastructure, and supporting operational processes and systems.

  • Understanding of deal / trade lifecycles (execution, settlement, pricing and valuations, reconciliations, investment reporting, performance measurement, fee and NAV calculation, risk measurement)

  • Knowledge of best practices related to cybersecurity, technology infrastructure, business continuity, disaster recovery planning.

  • Knowledge of regulatory environments for investment managers operating in major jurisdictions.

  • Extensive professional work experience (5+ years) postgraduate.

  • Direct experience in the alternative investment industry (hedge fund, private equity, venture capital, private credit) an asset (5+ years)

  • Investment operations, middle office, and/or fund accounting experience in a management role an asset (5+ years).  

  • Strong capability in evaluating and improving internal control processes.

  • Proficient in preparing formal documentation and presentations, as well as communicating materials to senior management.

  • Experience with change management, strategic planning, continuous improvement.

What we’re offering

The Base Salary range for this level is as follows: $105,000 - $155,000. Individual compensation and placement of this role in the range is based on different factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, demonstrated competencies, and internal equity as they relate to the role.

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Private Capital
jobs.description:
Our Company  

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board (Ontario Teachers') is the administrator of Canada's largest single-profession pension plan, with CAD $270 billion in net assets as of June 30, 2025. We hold a diverse global portfolio of assets, approximately 80% of which is managed in-house. Ontario Teachers’ invests and administers the pensions of the province of Ontario’s 343,000 active and retired teachers. Ontario Teachers’ is an independent organization headquartered in Toronto and our other investment offices are in London, Singapore, New York, Sao Paolo, Dallas, San Francisco and India. We employ more than 1,500 employees globally.   

OTPP manages ~$38B of assets out of the London office, of which ~C$8B is in the Private Capital portfolio. OTPP has been an investor in Europe since 1992, with the London office launched in 2007 to gain enhanced access to opportunities in EMEA.

Private Capital is the investment department within OTPP focused on private equity investing, with global Assets Under Management of $61B. We invest across three core sectors (Financial Services, Technology, and Services) and focus on control and co-control investments. Our European portfolio includes companies such as Busy Bees, 7IM, Donte Group, Max Matthiessen, Group.One and Veonet.

The Opportunity 

We are seeking an Associate for our Private Capital (“PC”) team. Based in London, you will be an integral part of the team focused on direct investments in Europe. You will play a key role in evaluating potential investment opportunities, executing transactions and handling existing portfolio investments that make up our European direct private equity investment strategy. 

What you'll do 

Your time will be spent evaluating new investments and determining whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity. Among other things, the analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company research, managing third-party advisors, building sophisticated financial models, reviewing and analysing due diligence materials, and preparing internal support materials and written recommendations to PC’s Investment Committee. 

Once a transaction has been completed, you will remain involved with the business, potentially attending board meetings as an observer, and will work with the management team and the board of directors to implement value-enhancing initiatives. In addition, you will work directly with management on various initiatives as required, including with respect to mergers and acquisitions, debt refinancings, budgeting and other strategic projects. 

You get to play an active role in assisting in and identifying target investment sectors and generating proprietary deal flow in specific sectors. Activities will include conducting desk research, working with bankers and consultants, and attending conferences. 

What you'll need 

  • An undergraduate degree  

  • 2-3 years relevant work experience – such as in private equity, investment banking, or consulting 

  • Very strong financial modelling and valuation skills 

  • Robust analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth, creative, critical analysis 

  • Deep understanding of fundamental investment analysis 

  • Ability to develop a good knowledge of the economic/business characteristics, conditions and trends of a broad range of industries 

  • Excellent communications skills, both oral and written, with solid presentation skills 

  • Can-do attitude, hunger to learn and grow, maturity and composure

  • Great teammate with strong interpersonal skills, able to establish and build relationships 

  • Self-motivated with the ability to meet tight timelines and operate in a highly complex and demanding work environment 

  • Unquestioned personal and professional integrity 

  • Fluency in English is essential. Other Western European languages considered an asset 

  • Ability and willingness to travel

jobs.title: Senior Manager, Valuation - 12-month contract
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Senior Manager, Valuation will be responsible for the execution of OTPP’s semi-annual private investment valuation processes, for the purpose of OTPP’s mid-year and year-end financial statement reporting. Private portfolio investments asset classes include private equity, infrastructure, natural resources, venture capital, real estate, and private credit. This position will also actively involve training and reviewing the work of junior staff. 

Who you'll work with

The Senior Manager will report to the Director, Valuation.

What you'll do

Leading the planning and execution of the mid-year and year-end valuation processes during peak periods, including:

  • Prepare and oversee the review of “fair value” valuations prepared by OTPP’s Investment teams including the preparation of analysis and summary memorandums in respect thereof

  • Developing an appropriate understanding of the assigned investments and remaining current on individual investment developments through participation in Investment team portfolio monitoring meetings

  • Review the fair value valuations prepared by external valuation agents

  • Develop recommendations of fair value to be used for the purposes of OTPP’s financial statements

  • Support the Director, Valuation in the development, tracking and reporting of plans and timelines to ensure an effective and efficient valuation process

  • Develop the standards for reviews and the corresponding processes to be followed by the staff involved in the valuation process

  • Respond to queries/information requests from OTPP’s external auditors with respect to the private investment valuations

  • Communicate noted issues and generate solutions with respect to the valuation process or specific investment valuations to the Director, Valuation on a timely basis

Interface with the Investment Division with respect to the valuations, including:

  • Coordinating processes and deliverables to facilitate the execution of a smooth mid-year and year-end valuation process

  • Communicating noted issues with respect to the process and/or issues identified with respect to specific investment valuations

  • Ensuring exceptional service is provided to the Investment Division with respect to private investment valuations 

  • Responding to inquiries and requests from the Investment Division including consultation on new transactions where required

Managing external advisor involvement in the mid-year and year-end valuation process, including:

  • Develop recommendations on the nature and use of external advisors including the selection of specific advisors and the scope of their review

  • Managing the entire process around the use of external advisors including:

  • Execution of terms of engagement with specific advisors

  • Establishing timelines, deliverables and reporting processes for external advisors

  • Ensuring internal information required by external advisors is provided by both the Investment Division and Finance in accordance with established timelines

  • Monitoring and reporting on progress of external advisor deliverables and associated costs

  • Review and incorporation of external advisor recommendations/findings into OTPP’s recommendations on fair value

  • Communicating noted issues, and generating solutions, to the Director, Valuation on a timely basis

  • Ensuring exceptional service is provided by external parties

Training, coaching and Supervision of junior staff, including:

  • Be involved in the hiring of junior staff

  • Develop training material for junior staff

  • Provide on the job coaching and train junior staff with respect to valuation standards, practices, and internal process requirements to ensure the execution of effective and efficient valuation processes

Ensure OTPP’s valuation policies and practices remain best practice, including:

  • Review and monitoring of external best practice valuation standards and practices

  • Be involved in the annual review and update of OTPP’s valuation policies including recommending changes based on changes in best practice valuation guidelines and practices

  • Prepare any required OTPP Board reporting with respect to any recommended process or policy changes

  • Contribute to OTPP Board reporting with respect to the results of the mid-year and year end private investment valuation processes

  • Contribute to ad-hoc projects supporting the execution of the broader Finance division strategy

What you'll need

  • Relevant university degree

  • A professional accounting designation (“CPA”), a Chartered Financial Analyst (“CFA”) and/or Chartered Business Valuator (“CBV”) designation

  • Valuation Knowledge – This position requires a solid understanding and application of the principles and complexities of private asset valuation. This includes experience in the application of various valuation approaches as it might relate to private equity, infrastructure, venture capital, natural resources, real estate, private debt, and other private investments.

  • Analytical Thinking and Financial Modelling Skills – Strong analytical thinking, variance analysis, and financial/cash flow modelling skills are important to the execution of the valuation review process.  Having a comfort level in reviewing large and complex financial models is important. 

  • Project Management Skills – OTPP’s valuation process involves the completion of an extensive number of valuation reviews and coordination across multiple departments and teams including within Finance.  Effective planning and project management skills to set priorities, coordinate tasks, supervise junior staff and meet deadlines are essential.

  • Written Communication Skills – This position requires the preparation of written memoranda supported by in-depth valuation analyses.  Strong written communication skills are required to ensure Finance’s memoranda are written clearly and concisely, address the key valuation risks identified and adequately support Finance’s recommendation of fair value.

  • Verbal Communication Skills – This position requires interaction with internal management and various OTPP Investment Division teams for the purpose of completing the valuation review. The nature of the relationship with these parties requires a high degree of professionalism.

  • Experience in private investment valuations (minimum 8 years)

  • Previous experience in coaching, training and developing staff

  • Experience interacting with senior management, external advisors and external auditors is critical

What we’re offering

  • Competitive compensation

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Principal - Funds, Teachers' Venture Growth
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Founded in 1990, Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan (OTPP) is one of Canada’s largest institutional investors, with C$279.4 billion in net assets as of December 31, 2025. Teachers’ Venture Growth (TVG) is an investment department within OTPP focused on late-stage venture and growth equity investments in technology-enabled businesses.

TVG employs two complementary investment strategies - direct investing and investing through funds - to deliver attractive risk-adjusted returns, access differentiated deal flow, and generate market insights. The TVG Funds portfolio consists of commitments to leading global venture and venture-growth managers, and continues to evolve as a core pillar of the broader TVG strategy.

As a Principal within TVG Funds team, you will play a key role in executing and managing TVG’s global funds strategy. You will contribute across the full lifecycle of fund investments, including origination, due diligence, execution, and ongoing portfolio management, while working closely with colleagues across TVG’s global offices.

Who you'll work with

You will work alongside a collaborative and high-performing global team of investment professionals across Toronto, San Francisco, London, and Mumbai.

This role will report to the Head of TVG Funds and partner closely with:

  • TVG Direct investment teams globally

  • Senior leadership across TVG

  • External fund managers (GPs), LP peers, and broader ecosystem participants

You will also work collaboratively across OTPP divisions to help drive enhanced sector and investment stage specialization and overall regional coordination. As a new department within Ontario Teachers', partnering with regional and asset class colleagues will be vital to success.

What you'll do

  • Evaluate new and follow-on fund investment opportunities across venture, venture-growth, and growth equity strategies

  • Lead and support due diligence processes, including GP assessment, track record analysis, and portfolio construction review

  • Conduct market mapping and manager research to inform investment decisions and sourcing efforts

  • Contribute to underwriting, structuring, and negotiation of fund commitments

  • Prepare and present investment materials for internal committees

  • Support ongoing monitoring of the global funds portfolio, including performance tracking, valuation, and risk management

  • Develop portfolio-level analytics and insights across returns, exposure, and strategic value (e.g., co-investment opportunities and sector insights)

  • Participate in LPACs, AGMs, and ongoing GP engagement

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with global GPs, emerging managers, and LP peers

  • Represent OTPP at industry events and within the venture ecosystem

  • Partner with TVG Direct teams to identify and evaluate shared investment opportunities and share market insights

  • Contribute to broader TVG strategic initiatives and knowledge-sharing efforts

  • Mentor and support junior team members, including providing feedback and development opportunities

  • Work cross-functionally and collaboratively with other teams to ensure continued alignment with OTPP’s mission, vision and values. This includes supporting and sharing knowledge with other areas of Investments and responding to ad hoc requests from other departments

What you'll need

  • Minimum 5+ years of relevant experience in venture capital, growth equity, fund investing, or related fields (e.g., investment banking or consulting with private markets exposure)

  • Experience evaluating or working with investment funds, including understanding of fund structures, economics, and portfolio construction

  • Strong analytical and financial modeling capabilities, with the ability to synthesize complex information into clear investment insights

  • Demonstrated ability to assess investment managers, strategies, and market positioning

  • Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills across global stakeholders

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and execute in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a high degree of ownership, curiosity, and accountability

  • Global perspective and strong interest in technology-driven sectors

  • Experience working across geographies and with cross-functional teams is an asset

  • Exposure to fund-of-funds, secondaries, or LP investing is an asset

  • CFA, MBA, or other relevant advanced degree is an asset

  • Advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint

  • Ability and willingness to travel as required

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience.
